<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./repos/shopsystem-messaging/src/shop_msg/cli.py`

```text
1	"""shop-msg CLI entry point.
2	
3	Subcommands:
4	    respond clarify --bc-root PATH --work-id ID --question TEXT
5	        Writes a Clarify message to the Postgres messages table as an
6	        outbox row. Raises on collision (duplicate bc/work_id/direction).
7	    respond work_done --bc-root PATH --work-id ID --status STATUS
8	                      [--scenario-hash HASH ...] [--summary TEXT]
9	        Writes a WorkDone message to the Postgres messages table.
10	    respond mechanism_observation --bc-root PATH --work-id ID --subject TEXT
11	                                  --body TEXT [--observed-during ID]
12	                                  [--evidence TEXT ...] [--proposed-action TEXT]
13	                                  [--provenance-ref REF]
14	        Writes a MechanismObservation message to the Postgres messages table.
15	        The optional --provenance-ref names a BC-side record (issue, document,
16	        commit) where long-form analysis lives; the wire schema does not
17	        constrain that reference to any particular tracker so the
18	        catalog stays decoupled from the BC's work-registry choice
19	        (lead-231 item C).
20	    send request_maintenance --bc-root PATH --work-id ID --description TEXT
21	                             [--acceptance-criterion TEXT ...]
22	                             [--file-hint TEXT ...]
23	        Writes a RequestMaintenance message to the Postgres messages table
24	        as an inbox row.
25	    send assign_scenarios --bc-root PATH --work-id ID --feature-title TEXT
26	                          --bc-tag NAME --scenario-file PATH ...
27	        Writes an AssignScenarios message to the Postgres messages table.
28	        Each --scenario-file becomes one ScenarioPayload. The hash for
29	        each scenario is computed by shelling out to the `scenarios hash`
30	        CLI (the canonicalization rule lives in the scenarios package,
31	        not here).
32	    send request_bugfix --bc-root PATH --work-id ID --description TEXT
33	                        [--feature-title TEXT --bc-tag NAME
34	                         --scenario-file PATH ...]
35	        Writes a RequestBugfix message to the Postgres messages table.
36	        Scenarios are optional.
37	    read outbox --bc-root PATH --work-id ID
38	        Reads the latest outbox row for a work_id from Postgres, validates
39	        it against the BCResponse union, and dumps the canonical YAML to
40	        stdout. Exits non-zero (with a stderr message) when no outbox
41	        row matches the work_id or validation fails.
42	    read inbox --bc-root PATH --work-id ID
43	        Reads the inbox row for a work_id from Postgres, validates it
44	        against the LeadMessage union, and dumps the canonical YAML to
45	        stdout. Exits non-zero (with a stderr message) when no inbox
46	        row matches the work_id or validation fails.
47	    pending inbox --bc-root PATH
48	        Enumerates inbox messages that have no matching outbox response
49	        via a Postgres query. Stdout is one line per pending message of
50	        the form "<work_id> <message_type>". Exit zero in both the
51	        empty and non-empty cases.
52	    pending outbox --lead-root PATH [--bc NAME]
53	        Lead-side counterpart: queries Postgres for outbox rows across
54	        sibling BC clones. With --bc NAME, scopes to a single BC; without
55	        it, every BC under repos/ is included.
56	    dump [--bc-root PATH] [--direction inbox|outbox] [--limit N]
57	        Operator debugging: dumps rows from the messages table to stdout
58	        as YAML. With --bc-root, scopes to that BC. With --direction,
59	        scopes to inbox or outbox. With --limit, caps the result count.
60	    prime --bc-root PATH
61	        Session-start orientation. Prints the current DSN, DB reachability,
62	        count and list of pending inbox messages, and a brief CLI reminder.
63	        Exits 0 when the DB is reachable; non-zero when unreachable.
64	    watch --bc-root PATH
65	        Monitor-compatible inbox watcher. Drains unprocessed inbox messages
66	        on startup (one '<work_id> <message_type>' line per pending item),
67	        emits a 'READY' sentinel line, then blocks on Postgres LISTEN waiting
68	        for new inbox inserts. Each NOTIFY produces one output line. Never
69	        exits under normal operation. Exits non-zero with a message naming
70	        the DSN when the database is unreachable at startup.
71	    watch --lead-root PATH
72	        Lead-side outbox watcher. Discovers BC repos under <lead-root>/repos/,
73	        drains existing outbox rows on startup, emits a 'READY' sentinel line,
74	        then blocks on Postgres LISTEN for new outbox inserts across all BCs.
75	        Each NOTIFY produces one '<work_id> <message_type>' output line.
76	    consume outbox --bc-root PATH --work-id ID --message-type TYPE
77	        Lead-side consumption: marks a specific outbox row (identified by BC,
78	        work_id, and message_type) as consumed so it no longer appears in
79	        'pending outbox' output. Exits zero on success; exits non-zero with a
80	        descriptive stderr message when no matching unconsumed outbox row exists.
81	"""
82	from __future__ import annotations
83	
84	import argparse
85	import subprocess
86	import sys
87	from pathlib import Path
88	
89	import yaml
90	
91	from pydantic import TypeAdapter, ValidationError
92	
93	from catalog.schemas import (
94	    AssignScenarios,
95	    BCResponse,
96	    Clarify,
97	    LeadMessage,
98	    MechanismObservation,
99	    RequestBugfix,
100	    RequestMaintenance,
101	    ScenarioPayload,
102	    WorkDone,
103	)
104	from shop_msg.storage import (
105	    CollisionError,
106	    consume_outbox_message,
107	    delete_bc_messages,
108	    inbox_row_exists,
109	    insert_bc_response,
110	    insert_message,
111	    insert_raw_payload,
112	    outbox_row_exists,
113	    query_pending_inbox,
114	    query_pending_lead_inbox,
115	    query_pending_outbox,
116	    read_inbox_message,
117	    read_lead_inbox_message,
118	    read_outbox_messages,
119	    registry_add,
120	    registry_list,
121	    registry_remove,
122	    registry_sync,
123	    resolve_lead_shop,
124	    resolve_shop_name,
125	    watch_inbox,
126	    watch_lead_inbox,
127	    watch_outbox_for_lead,
128	)
129	
130	_response_adapter = TypeAdapter(BCResponse)
131	_lead_adapter = TypeAdapter(LeadMessage)
132	
133	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
134	# Migration guards for removed flags
135	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
136	
137	
138	class _RemovedFlagAction(argparse.Action):
139	    """argparse Action that immediately errors out with a migration message."""
140	
141	    def __call__(self, parser, namespace, values, option_string=None):
142	        flag = option_string or self.option_strings[0]
143	        if flag == "--bc-root":
144	            replacement = "--bc <name>"
145	        elif flag == "--lead-root":
146	            replacement = "--lead <name>"
147	        else:
148	            replacement = "--bc <name> or --lead <name>"
149	        print(
150	            f"shop-msg: {flag} is no longer supported. "
151	            f"Use {replacement} instead (name-based addressing, PDR-007).",
152	            file=sys.stderr,
153	        )
154	        sys.exit(1)
155	
156	
157	def _add_removed_flag(parser: argparse.ArgumentParser, flag: str) -> None:
158	    """Register a removed flag so it produces a migration error instead of
159	    'unrecognized arguments'."""
160	    parser.add_argument(
161	        flag,
162	        nargs="?",
163	        action=_RemovedFlagAction,
164	        help=argparse.SUPPRESS,
165	    )
166	
167	
168	def _resolve_bc(args: argparse.Namespace) -> str:
169	    """Resolve --bc <name> to the bc_root path via the registry.
170	
171	    Exits non-zero if the name is not registered.
172	    """
173	    name = args.bc
174	    path = resolve_shop_name(name)
175	    if path is None:
176	        print(
177	            f"shop-msg: shop name {name!r} is not registered in the registry. "
178	            f"Run 'shop-msg registry add' to register it first.",
179	            file=sys.stderr,
180	        )
181	        sys.exit(1)
182	    return str(Path(path).resolve())
183	
184	
185	def _resolve_lead(args: argparse.Namespace) -> str:
186	    """Resolve --lead <name> to the lead_root path via the registry.
187	
188	    Exits non-zero if the name is not registered.
189	    """
190	    name = args.lead
191	    path = resolve_shop_name(name)
192	    if path is None:
193	        print(
194	            f"shop-msg: lead name {name!r} is not registered in the registry. "
195	            f"Run 'shop-msg registry add' to register it first.",
196	            file=sys.stderr,
197	        )
198	        sys.exit(1)
199	    return str(Path(path).resolve())
200	
201	
202	def _resolve_registered_lead() -> str | None:
203	    """Return the shop_root for the registered lead shop, or None.
204	
205	    Used by ``respond`` commands to route responses to the lead's inbox.
206	    """
207	    path = resolve_lead_shop()
208	    if path is None:
209	        return None
210	    return str(Path(path).resolve())
211	
212	
213	def _compute_scenario_hash(gherkin_body: str) -> str:
214	    """Shell out to `scenarios hash` to canonicalize and hash a scenario body.
215	
216	    The package boundary is intentional: the canonicalization rule belongs
217	    to the scenarios package, and shop-msg composes it as an external tool
218	    just like any other consumer would.
219	    """
220	    result = subprocess.run(
221	        ["scenarios", "hash"],
222	        input=gherkin_body,
223	        capture_output=True,
224	        text=True,
225	        check=True,
226	    )
227	    return result.stdout.strip()
228	
229	
230	def _cmd_respond_clarify(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
231	    bc_root = _resolve_bc(args)
232	
233	    if not args.question:
234	        print(
235	            "shop-msg respond clarify: question must not be empty",
236	            file=sys.stderr,
237	        )
238	        return 1
239	
240	    if not args.work_id or "/" in args.work_id or ".." in args.work_id:
241	        print(
242	            "shop-msg respond clarify: refusing unsafe work_id",
243	            file=sys.stderr,
244	        )
245	        return 1
246	
247	    lead_root = _resolve_registered_lead()
248	    if lead_root is None:
249	        print(
250	            "shop-msg respond clarify: no lead shop is registered in the registry. "
251	            "Run 'shop-msg registry add --lead-shop' to register the lead first.",
252	            file=sys.stderr,
253	        )
254	        return 1
255	
256	    message = Clarify(
257	        message_type="clarify",
258	        work_id=args.work_id,
259	        question=args.question,
260	    )
261	
262	    try:
263	        insert_bc_response(
264	            lead_root,
265	            bc_root,
266	            args.work_id,
267	            "clarify",
268	            message.model_dump(),
269	        )
270	    except CollisionError:
271	        print(
272	            f"shop-msg respond clarify: refusing to overwrite existing response "
273	            f"for work_id={args.work_id!r}",
274	            file=sys.stderr,
275	        )
276	        return 1
277	    return 0
278	
279	
280	def _cmd_respond_work_done(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
281	    bc_root = _resolve_bc(args)
282	
283	    lead_root = _resolve_registered_lead()
284	    if lead_root is None:
285	        print(
286	            "shop-msg respond work_done: no lead shop is registered in the registry. "
287	            "Run 'shop-msg registry add --lead-shop' to register the lead first.",
288	            file=sys.stderr,
289	        )
290	        return 1
291	
292	    message = WorkDone(
293	        message_type="work_done",
294	        work_id=args.work_id,
295	        status=args.status,
296	        summary=args.summary,
297	        scenario_hashes=list(args.scenario_hash or []),
298	    )
299	
300	    try:
301	        insert_bc_response(
302	            lead_root,
303	            bc_root,
304	            args.work_id,
305	            "work_done",
306	            message.model_dump(),
307	        )
308	    except CollisionError:
309	        print(
310	            f"shop-msg respond work_done: refusing to overwrite existing response "
311	            f"for work_id={args.work_id!r}",
312	            file=sys.stderr,
313	        )
314	        return 1
315	    return 0
316	
317	
318	def _cmd_respond_mechanism_observation(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
319	    bc_root = _resolve_bc(args)
320	
321	    # Path-safety: refuse work_ids that would escape safe naming.
322	    if "/" in args.work_id or ".." in args.work_id or not args.work_id:
323	        print(
324	            f"shop-msg respond mechanism_observation: refusing unsafe "
325	            f"work_id {args.work_id!r}",
326	            file=sys.stderr,
327	        )
328	        return 1
329	
330	    lead_root = _resolve_registered_lead()
331	    if lead_root is None:
332	        print(
333	            "shop-msg respond mechanism_observation: no lead shop is registered "
334	            "in the registry. Run 'shop-msg registry add --lead-shop' first.",
335	            file=sys.stderr,
336	        )
337	        return 1
338	
339	    message = MechanismObservation(
340	        message_type="mechanism_observation",
341	        subject=args.subject,
342	        observed_during=args.observed_during,
343	        body=args.body,
344	        evidence=list(args.evidence) if args.evidence else None,
345	        proposed_action=args.proposed_action,
346	        provenance_ref=args.provenance_ref,
347	    )
348	
349	    try:
350	        insert_bc_response(
351	            lead_root,
352	            bc_root,
353	            args.work_id,
354	            "mechanism_observation",
355	            message.model_dump(exclude_none=True),
356	        )
357	    except CollisionError:
358	        print(
359	            f"shop-msg respond mechanism_observation: refusing to overwrite "
360	            f"existing response for work_id={args.work_id!r}",
361	            file=sys.stderr,
362	        )
363	        return 1
364	    return 0
365	
366	
367	def _cmd_send_request_maintenance(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
368	    bc_root = _resolve_bc(args)
369	
370	    acceptance_criteria = list(args.acceptance_criterion or []) or None
371	    file_hints = list(args.file_hint or []) or None
372	
373	    message = RequestMaintenance(
374	        message_type="request_maintenance",
375	        work_id=args.work_id,
376	        description=args.description,
377	        acceptance_criteria=acceptance_criteria,
378	        file_hints=file_hints,
379	    )
380	
381	    try:
382	        insert_message(
383	            bc_root,
384	            args.work_id,
385	            "inbox",
386	            "request_maintenance",
387	            message.model_dump(exclude_none=True),
388	            notify=True,
389	        )
390	    except CollisionError:
391	        print(
392	            f"shop-msg send request_maintenance: refusing to overwrite existing "
393	            f"inbox entry for work_id={args.work_id!r}",
394	            file=sys.stderr,
395	        )
396	        return 1
397	    return 0
398	
399	
400	def _build_scenario_payload(
401	    path_str: str, feature_title: str, bc_tag: str
402	) -> ScenarioPayload:
403	    """Read a scenario body file, wrap it with the standard Feature header
404	    and tags, and hash the wrapped result. Shared between assign_scenarios
405	    and request_bugfix because both messages embed the same
406	    ScenarioPayload shape.
407	
408	    lead-018 tightened the ScenarioPayload schema so that
409	    `hash == canonical_hash(gherkin)`. The `gherkin` field stores the
410	    wrapped text (Feature header + tag line + body), so we must hash the
411	    wrapped text — hashing the inner body alone would put the CLI on the
412	    wrong side of the schema invariant.
413	
414	    Because the canonicalization rule strips `@scenario_hash:` lines
415	    before hashing, the chicken-and-egg of "the tag line includes the
416	    hash we're about to compute" resolves naturally: we can construct
417	    the wrapped text using the eventual hash as a placeholder, hash it,
418	    then replace the placeholder — but the simpler form below works
419	    because the canonicalization strips `@scenario_hash:<anything>`
420	    lines unconditionally. So we build the wrapped text with a sentinel
421	    hash, compute the canonical hash of that string (the sentinel line
422	    is dropped by canonicalization), and emit the same wrapped string
423	    with the now-known hash substituted in. The substitution does not
424	    perturb the canonical hash because both forms differ only in the
425	    stripped `@scenario_hash:` line.
426	    """
427	    body = Path(path_str).read_text()
428	    sentinel = "0" * 16
429	    sentinel_tags = [f"@scenario_hash:{sentinel}", f"@bc:{bc_tag}"]
430	    sentinel_tagged = (
431	        f"Feature: {feature_title}\n"
432	        f"\n"
433	        f"  {' '.join(sentinel_tags)}\n"
434	        f"  {body}\n"
435	    )
436	    scen_hash = _compute_scenario_hash(sentinel_tagged)
437	    tags = [f"@scenario_hash:{scen_hash}", f"@bc:{bc_tag}"]
438	    tagged = (
439	        f"Feature: {feature_title}\n"
440	        f"\n"
441	        f"  {' '.join(tags)}\n"
442	        f"  {body}\n"
443	    )
444	    return ScenarioPayload(hash=scen_hash, tags=tags, gherkin=tagged)
445	
446	
447	def _cmd_send_request_bugfix(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
448	    bc_root = _resolve_bc(args)
449	
450	    scenario_files = list(args.scenario_file or [])
451	    # --feature-title and --bc-tag are conditionally required: only when
452	    # at least one --scenario-file is supplied. argparse cannot express
453	    # "required iff" directly, so enforce post-parse here.
454	    if scenario_files and (args.feature_title is None or args.bc_tag is None):
455	        print(
456	            "shop-msg send request_bugfix: --feature-title and --bc-tag are "
457	            "required when --scenario-file is supplied",
458	            file=sys.stderr,
459	        )
460	        return 2
461	
462	    scenarios_payload: list[ScenarioPayload] = [
463	        _build_scenario_payload(path_str, args.feature_title, args.bc_tag)
464	        for path_str in scenario_files
465	    ]
466	
467	    message = RequestBugfix(
468	        message_type="request_bugfix",
469	        work_id=args.work_id,
470	        description=args.description,
471	        scenarios=scenarios_payload,
472	    )
473	
474	    try:
475	        insert_message(
476	            bc_root,
477	            args.work_id,
478	            "inbox",
479	            "request_bugfix",
480	            message.model_dump(),
481	            notify=True,
482	        )
483	    except CollisionError:
484	        print(
485	            f"shop-msg send request_bugfix: refusing to overwrite existing "
486	            f"inbox entry for work_id={args.work_id!r}",
487	            file=sys.stderr,
488	        )
489	        return 1
490	    return 0
491	
492	
493	def _cmd_send_assign_scenarios(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
494	    bc_root = _resolve_bc(args)
495	
496	    scenario_files = list(args.scenario_file or [])
497	    scenarios_payload: list[ScenarioPayload] = [
498	        _build_scenario_payload(path_str, args.feature_title, args.bc_tag)
499	        for path_str in scenario_files
500	    ]
501	
502	    message = AssignScenarios(
503	        message_type="assign_scenarios",
504	        work_id=args.work_id,
505	        scenarios=scenarios_payload,
506	    )
507	
508	    try:
509	        insert_message(
510	            bc_root,
511	            args.work_id,
512	            "inbox",
513	            "assign_scenarios",
514	            message.model_dump(),
515	            notify=True,
516	        )
517	    except CollisionError:
518	        print(
519	            f"shop-msg send assign_scenarios: refusing to overwrite existing "
520	            f"inbox entry for work_id={args.work_id!r}",
521	            file=sys.stderr,
522	        )
523	        return 1
524	    return 0
525	
526	
527	def _cmd_read_outbox(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
528	    bc_root = _resolve_bc(args)
529	    rows = read_outbox_messages(bc_root, args.work_id)
530	    if not rows:
531	        print(
532	            f"shop-msg read outbox: no outbox response found for "
533	            f"work_id={args.work_id!r} in bc={bc_root!r}",
534	            file=sys.stderr,
535	        )
536	        return 1
537	    # Use the most-recently-inserted row (last in created_at order).
538	    raw = rows[-1]
539	    try:
540	        message = _response_adapter.validate_python(raw)
541	    except ValidationError as e:
542	        print(
543	            f"shop-msg read outbox: validation failed for "
544	            f"work_id={args.work_id!r}:\n{e}",
545	            file=sys.stderr,
546	        )
547	        return 1
548	    print(f"valid {message.message_type} from {args.work_id}:")
549	    print(yaml.safe_dump(message.model_dump(exclude_none=True), sort_keys=False))
550	    return 0
551	
552	
553	def _cmd_read_inbox(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
554	    bc_root = _resolve_bc(args)
555	    raw = read_inbox_message(bc_root, args.work_id)
556	    if raw is None:
557	        # Same pattern as `read outbox`: a missing row is the
558	        # caller's mistake, not a schema problem; surface a phrase the
559	        # step definitions can substring-check ("no inbox message").
560	        print(
561	            f"shop-msg read inbox: no inbox message found for "
562	            f"work_id={args.work_id!r} in bc={bc_root!r}",
563	            file=sys.stderr,
564	        )
565	        return 1
566	    try:
567	        message = _lead_adapter.validate_python(raw)
568	    except ValidationError as e:
569	        # The "validation failed" phrase is load-bearing — the step
570	        # definition for the schema-validation scenario substring-checks
571	        # it. Keep the wording aligned with `read outbox`'s sibling
572	        # branch so a future consolidation doesn't drift one without
573	        # the other.
574	        print(
575	            f"shop-msg read inbox: validation failed for "
576	            f"work_id={args.work_id!r}:\n{e}",
577	            file=sys.stderr,
578	        )
579	        return 1
580	    print(f"valid {message.message_type} from {args.work_id}.yaml:")
581	    print(yaml.safe_dump(message.model_dump(exclude_none=True), sort_keys=False))
582	    return 0
583	
584	
585	def _cmd_pending_inbox(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
586	    """Enumerate inbox messages that have no matching outbox response.
587	
588	    Queries Postgres using the pending-query SQL that replaces the old
589	    directory-glob walk. Missing bc_root directories are no longer
590	    relevant — the database is the store.
591	    """
592	    bc_root = _resolve_bc(args)
593	    rows = query_pending_inbox(bc_root)
594	    for work_id, message_type in rows:
595	        print(f"{work_id} {message_type}")
596	    return 0
597	
598	
599	def _cmd_pending_lead_inbox(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
600	    """Enumerate BC responses in the lead's inbox.
601	
602	    Lead-side counterpart to ``pending inbox --bc``.  Queries Postgres for
603	    inbox rows stored under the lead's namespace — these are BC responses
604	    that arrived via ``shop-msg respond`` under the new routing model
605	    (Brief-006 scope C / lead-e9x).
606	    """
607	    lead_root = _resolve_lead(args)
608	    rows = query_pending_lead_inbox(lead_root)
609	    for work_id, message_type in rows:
610	        print(f"{work_id} {message_type}")
611	    return 0
612	
613	
614	def _cmd_read_lead_inbox(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
615	    """Read and validate a BC response from the lead's inbox.
616	
617	    Lead-side counterpart to ``read inbox --bc``.  Reads the row stored
618	    under the lead's namespace for the given work_id and validates it
619	    against the BCResponse schema union.
620	    """
621	    lead_root = _resolve_lead(args)
622	    raw = read_lead_inbox_message(lead_root, args.work_id)
623	    if raw is None:
624	        print(
625	            f"shop-msg read inbox: no inbox message found for "
626	            f"work_id={args.work_id!r} in lead={lead_root!r}",
627	            file=sys.stderr,
628	        )
629	        return 1
630	    try:
631	        message = _response_adapter.validate_python(raw)
632	    except ValidationError as e:
633	        print(
634	            f"shop-msg read inbox: validation failed for "
635	            f"work_id={args.work_id!r}:\n{e}",
636	            file=sys.stderr,
637	        )
638	        return 1
639	    print(f"valid {message.message_type} from {args.work_id}:")
640	    print(yaml.safe_dump(message.model_dump(exclude_none=True), sort_keys=False))
641	    return 0
642	
643	
644	def _cmd_pending_outbox(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
645	    """Enumerate pending outbox responses across sibling BC clones.
646	
647	    Lead-side counterpart to `pending inbox`. Queries Postgres for
648	    outbox rows whose bc path sits under <lead-root>/repos/.
649	    """
650	    lead_root = _resolve_lead(args)
651	    rows = query_pending_outbox(lead_root, bc_filter=args.bc_name)
652	    for work_id, message_type, bc_name in rows:
653	        print(f"{work_id} {message_type} {bc_name}")
654	    return 0
655	
656	
657	def _cmd_consume_outbox(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
658	    """Mark a specific outbox row as consumed (lead side).
659	
660	    After consumption the row no longer appears in 'pending outbox' output.
661	    Exits non-zero if no matching unconsumed outbox row exists.
662	    """
663	    bc_root = _resolve_bc(args)
664	    found = consume_outbox_message(bc_root, args.work_id, args.message_type)
665	    if not found:
666	        print(
667	            f"shop-msg consume outbox: no unconsumed outbox row found for "
668	            f"work_id={args.work_id!r} message_type={args.message_type!r} "
669	            f"in bc={bc_root!r}",
670	            file=sys.stderr,
671	        )
672	        return 1
673	    return 0
674	
675	
676	def _cmd_prime(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
677	    """Session-start orientation for BC agents.
678	
679	    Prints:
680	      1. Current DSN
681	      2. DB reachability (yes / no — <error>)
682	      3. Count of unprocessed inbox messages
683	      4. Each pending work_id + message_type
684	      5. A brief reminder block about the CLI
685	
686	    Exit 0 when DB is reachable; non-zero when unreachable.
687	    """
688	    from shop_msg.storage import _get_dsn, query_pending_inbox
689	
690	    bc_root = _resolve_bc(args)
691	    bc_name = args.bc
692	    dsn = _get_dsn()
693	
694	    print(f"DSN: {dsn}")
695	
696	    # Probe connectivity.
697	    try:
698	        pending_rows = query_pending_inbox(bc_root)
699	        db_reachable = True
700	    except Exception as exc:
701	        print(f"DB reachable: no — {exc}")
702	        print()
703	        _print_prime_reminder(bc_name)
704	        return 1
705	
706	    print("DB reachable: yes")
707	    print()
708	
709	    count = len(pending_rows)
710	    print(f"Pending inbox messages: {count}")
711	    for work_id, message_type in pending_rows:
712	        print(f"  {work_id}  {message_type}")
713	    print()
714	
715	    _print_prime_reminder(bc_name)
716	    return 0
717	
718	
719	def _print_prime_reminder(bc_name: str) -> None:
720	    print(
721	        "Use shop-msg CLI commands — inbox/outbox are in postgres, not on the filesystem.\n"
722	        "Key commands:\n"
723	        f"  shop-msg pending inbox --bc {bc_name}\n"
724	        f"  shop-msg read inbox --bc {bc_name} --work-id <id>\n"
725	        "  shop-msg respond clarify | work_done | mechanism_observation ..."
726	    )
727	
728	
729	def _cmd_prime_lead(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
730	    """Session-start orientation for lead shop agents.
731	
732	    Prints:
733	      1. Current DSN
734	      2. DB reachability (yes / no — <error>)
735	      3. Count of unconsumed BC responses in the lead's inbox
736	      4. Each pending work_id + message_type
737	      5. A brief reminder block with key commands for the lead role
738	
739	    Exit 0 when DB is reachable; non-zero when unreachable.
740	    """
741	    from shop_msg.storage import _get_dsn, query_pending_lead_inbox
742	
743	    lead_root = _resolve_lead(args)
744	    lead_name = args.lead
745	    dsn = _get_dsn()
746	
747	    print(f"DSN: {dsn}")
748	
749	    # Probe connectivity.
750	    try:
751	        pending_rows = query_pending_lead_inbox(lead_root)
752	        db_reachable = True
753	    except Exception as exc:
754	        print(f"DB reachable: no — {exc}")
755	        print()
756	        _print_prime_lead_reminder(lead_name)
757	        return 1
758	
759	    print("DB reachable: yes")
760	    print()
761	
762	    count = len(pending_rows)
763	    print(f"Pending outbox responses: {count}")
764	    for work_id, message_type in pending_rows:
765	        print(f"  {work_id}  {message_type}")
766	    print()
767	
768	    _print_prime_lead_reminder(lead_name)
769	    return 0
770	
771	
772	def _print_prime_lead_reminder(lead_name: str) -> None:
773	    print(
774	        "Use shop-msg CLI commands — inbox/outbox are in postgres, not on the filesystem.\n"
775	        "Key commands:\n"
776	        f"  shop-msg pending inbox --lead {lead_name}\n"
777	        f"  shop-msg read inbox --lead {lead_name} --work-id <id>\n"
778	        "  shop-msg respond clarify  # lead answers BC questions\n"
779	        "  shop-msg respond work_done | mechanism_observation ..."
780	    )
781	
782	
783	def _cmd_prime_dispatch(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
784	    """Dispatch prime to BC mode (--bc) or lead mode (--lead)."""
785	    if getattr(args, "lead", None):
786	        return _cmd_prime_lead(args)
787	    return _cmd_prime(args)
788	
789	
790	def _cmd_dump(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
791	    """Operator debugging: dump rows from the messages table."""
792	    import json as _json
793	    from shop_msg.storage import _connect, _bc_id
794	
795	    bc_root: str | None = None
796	    if hasattr(args, "bc") and args.bc:
797	        bc_root = resolve_shop_name(args.bc)
798	        if bc_root is None:
799	            print(
800	                f"shop-msg dump: shop name {args.bc!r} not registered in registry",
801	                file=sys.stderr,
802	            )
803	            return 1
804	        bc_root = str(Path(bc_root).resolve())
805	    direction = args.direction
806	    limit = args.limit or 100
807	
808	    with _connect() as conn:
809	        with conn.cursor() as cur:
810	            conditions = []
811	            params: list = []
812	            if bc_root is not None:
813	                conditions.append("bc = %s")
814	                params.append(_bc_id(bc_root))
815	            if direction is not None:
816	                conditions.append("direction = %s")
817	                params.append(direction)
818	            where = ("WHERE " + " AND ".join(conditions)) if conditions else ""
819	            cur.execute(
820	                f"SELECT id, bc, work_id, direction, message_type, payload, created_at "
821	                f"FROM messages {where} ORDER BY created_at LIMIT %s",
822	                params + [limit],
823	            )
824	            rows = cur.fetchall()
825	
826	    for row in rows:
827	        print(yaml.safe_dump(dict(row), sort_keys=False, default_flow_style=False))
828	        print("---")
829	    return 0
830	
831	
832	def _cmd_watch(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
833	    """Drain pending messages then LISTEN for new ones.
834	
835	    Each line emitted to stdout is of the form:
836	        <work_id> <message_type>
837	
838	    A special "READY" sentinel line is emitted after the startup drain and
839	    before entering the LISTEN loop so that callers (e.g. the Monitor harness
840	    or BDD step definitions) can reliably detect when the drain phase is done.
841	
842	    Exits non-zero with a descriptive message to stderr if the database is
843	    unreachable at startup.
844	
845	    Modes:
846	      --bc <name>    Inbox watcher for a single BC (resolves via registry).
847	      --lead <name>  Outbox watcher across all BCs under the lead root.
848	    """
849	    try:
850	        if hasattr(args, "lead") and args.lead is not None:
851	            lead_root = _resolve_lead(args)
852	            watch_lead_inbox(lead_root)
853	            return 0
854	
855	        bc_root = _resolve_bc(args)
856	        watch_inbox(bc_root)
857	        return 0
858	    except RuntimeError as exc:
859	        print(str(exc), file=sys.stderr)
860	        return 1
861	
862	
863	def _cmd_registry_add(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
864	    """Register a shop by canonical name."""
865	    shop_type = "lead" if getattr(args, "lead_shop", False) else "bc"
866	    registry_add(args.name, args.shop_root, shop_type=shop_type)
867	    return 0
868	
869	
870	def _cmd_registry_remove(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
871	    """Remove a shop from the registry by canonical name."""
872	    found = registry_remove(args.name)
873	    if not found:
874	        print(
875	            f"shop-msg registry remove: {args.name!r} was not found in the registry",
876	            file=sys.stdout,
877	        )
878	    return 0
879	
880	
881	def _cmd_registry_list(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
882	    """List all registered shops."""
883	    entries = registry_list()
884	    for name, shop_root, shop_type in entries:
885	        print(f"{name} {shop_root} {shop_type}")
886	    return 0
887	
888	
889	def _cmd_registry_sync(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
890	    """Synchronise the registry from a BC manifest file."""
891	    registry_sync(args.manifest)
892	    return 0
893	
894	
895	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
896	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="shop-msg")
897	    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", required=True)
898	
899	    respond = sub.add_parser("respond", help="write a BC response message")
900	    respond_sub = respond.add_subparsers(dest="response_type", required=True)
901	
902	    clarify = respond_sub.add_parser("clarify", help="write a clarify response")
903	    clarify.add_argument("--bc", required=True, help="canonical BC name (resolved via registry)")
904	    _add_removed_flag(clarify, "--bc-root")
905	    clarify.add_argument("--work-id", required=True, help="work_id from the lead message")
906	    clarify.add_argument("--question", required=True, help="clarifying question text")
907	    clarify.set_defaults(func=_cmd_respond_clarify)
908	
909	    work_done = respond_sub.add_parser("work_done", help="write a work_done response")
910	    work_done.add_argument("--bc", required=True, help="canonical BC name (resolved via registry)")
911	    _add_removed_flag(work_done, "--bc-root")
912	    work_done.add_argument("--work-id", required=True, help="work_id from the lead message")
913	    work_done.add_argument(
914	        "--status",
915	        required=True,
916	        choices=["complete", "partial", "blocked"],
917	        help="work_done status",
918	    )
919	    work_done.add_argument(
920	        "--scenario-hash",
921	        action="append",
922	        default=None,
923	        help="scenario hash echoed back to the lead (repeatable)",
924	    )
925	    work_done.add_argument(
926	        "--summary",
927	        default=None,
928	        help="optional free-form summary of what was done",
929	    )
930	    work_done.set_defaults(func=_cmd_respond_work_done)
931	
932	    mech_obs = respond_sub.add_parser(
933	        "mechanism_observation",
934	        help="surface a BC observation about the shop-system mechanism",
935	    )
936	    mech_obs.add_argument("--bc", required=True, help="canonical BC name (resolved via registry)")
937	    _add_removed_flag(mech_obs, "--bc-root")
938	    mech_obs.add_argument(
939	        "--work-id", required=True,
940	        help=(
941	            "work_id naming this response in the BC's outbox. Mirrors the "
942	            "respond clarify / respond work_done flag of the same name."
943	        ),
944	    )
945	    mech_obs.add_argument(
946	        "--subject", required=True,
947	        help="one-line summary of the mechanism observation",
948	    )
949	    mech_obs.add_argument(
950	        "--body", required=True,
951	        help="markdown body: what was observed and why it's load-bearing",
952	    )
953	    mech_obs.add_argument(
954	        "--observed-during", default=None,
955	        help="lead-issued work_id the BC was working when this surfaced (optional)",
956	    )
957	    mech_obs.add_argument(
958	        "--evidence", action="append", default=None,
959	        help="verifiable pointer (file:line, template ref, package name); repeatable",
960	    )
961	    mech_obs.add_argument(
962	        "--proposed-action", default=None,
963	        help="BC's hypothesis for what to change (optional)",
964	    )
965	    mech_obs.add_argument(
966	        "--provenance-ref", default=None,
967	        help=(
968	            "optional, tracker-neutral pointer to a BC-side record where "
969	            "long-form analysis lives (e.g. an issue id, a doc path). The "
970	            "wire schema does not constrain this to any particular "
971	            "tracker (lead-231 item C)."
972	        ),
973	    )
974	    mech_obs.set_defaults(func=_cmd_respond_mechanism_observation)
975	
976	    send = sub.add_parser("send", help="write a lead-to-BC message into a BC's inbox")
977	    send_sub = send.add_subparsers(dest="message_type", required=True)
978	
979	    request_maintenance = send_sub.add_parser(
980	        "request_maintenance", help="write a request_maintenance message"
981	    )
982	    request_maintenance.add_argument("--bc", required=True, help="canonical BC name (resolved via registry)")
983	    _add_removed_flag(request_maintenance, "--bc-root")
984	    request_maintenance.add_argument(
985	        "--work-id", required=True, help="work_id identifying this assignment"
986	    )
987	    request_maintenance.add_argument(
988	        "--description", required=True, help="description of the work being requested"
989	    )
990	    request_maintenance.add_argument(
991	        "--acceptance-criterion",
992	        action="append",
993	        default=None,
994	        help="measurable acceptance criterion (repeatable)",
995	    )
996	    request_maintenance.add_argument(
997	        "--file-hint",
998	        action="append",
999	        default=None,
1000	        help="file path hint relevant to the work (repeatable)",
1001	    )
1002	    request_maintenance.set_defaults(func=_cmd_send_request_maintenance)
1003	
1004	    assign_scenarios = send_sub.add_parser(
1005	        "assign_scenarios", help="write an assign_scenarios message"
1006	    )
1007	    assign_scenarios.add_argument("--bc", required=True, help="canonical BC name (resolved via registry)")
1008	    _add_removed_flag(assign_scenarios, "--bc-root")
1009	    assign_scenarios.add_argument(
1010	        "--work-id", required=True, help="work_id identifying this assignment"
1011	    )
1012	    assign_scenarios.add_argument(
1013	        "--feature-title",
1014	        required=True,
1015	        help="title used in the wrapping `Feature:` line for each scenario",
1016	    )
1017	    assign_scenarios.add_argument(
1018	        "--bc-tag",
1019	        required=True,
1020	        help="BC name used in the @bc:<name> scenario tag",
1021	    )
1022	    assign_scenarios.add_argument(
1023	        "--scenario-file",
1024	        action="append",
1025	        default=None,
1026	        required=True,
1027	        help="path to a file containing one scenario body (repeatable)",
1028	    )
1029	    assign_scenarios.set_defaults(func=_cmd_send_assign_scenarios)
1030	
1031	    request_bugfix = send_sub.add_parser(
1032	        "request_bugfix", help="write a request_bugfix message"
1033	    )
1034	    request_bugfix.add_argument("--bc", required=True, help="canonical BC name (resolved via registry)")
1035	    _add_removed_flag(request_bugfix, "--bc-root")
1036	    request_bugfix.add_argument(
1037	        "--work-id", required=True, help="work_id identifying this assignment"
1038	    )
1039	    request_bugfix.add_argument(
1040	        "--description",
1041	        required=True,
1042	        help="plain-language description of the fix",
1043	    )
1044	    request_bugfix.add_argument(
1045	        "--feature-title",
1046	        default=None,
1047	        help=(
1048	            "title used in the wrapping `Feature:` line for each scenario; "
1049	            "required iff at least one --scenario-file is supplied"
1050	        ),
1051	    )
1052	    request_bugfix.add_argument(
1053	        "--bc-tag",
1054	        default=None,
1055	        help=(
1056	            "BC name used in the @bc:<name> scenario tag; required iff "
1057	            "at least one --scenario-file is supplied"
1058	        ),
1059	    )
1060	    request_bugfix.add_argument(
1061	        "--scenario-file",
1062	        action="append",
1063	        default=None,
1064	        help="path to a file containing one scenario body (repeatable, optional)",
1065	    )
1066	    request_bugfix.set_defaults(func=_cmd_send_request_bugfix)
1067	
1068	    read = sub.add_parser("read", help="read a message from a BC's mailboxes")
1069	    read_sub = read.add_subparsers(dest="read_target", required=True)
1070	
1071	    read_outbox = read_sub.add_parser(
1072	        "outbox", help="read and validate a BC response from its outbox"
1073	    )
1074	    read_outbox.add_argument("--bc", required=True, help="canonical BC name (resolved via registry)")
1075	    _add_removed_flag(read_outbox, "--bc-root")
1076	    read_outbox.add_argument(
1077	        "--work-id", required=True, help="work_id whose response to read"
1078	    )
1079	    read_outbox.set_defaults(func=_cmd_read_outbox)
1080	
1081	    read_inbox = read_sub.add_parser(
1082	        "inbox",
1083	        help=(
1084	            "read and validate a message from a BC's inbox (--bc) "
1085	            "or a BC response from the lead's inbox (--lead)"
1086	        ),
1087	    )
1088	    _read_inbox_mode = read_inbox.add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)
1089	    _read_inbox_mode.add_argument(
1090	        "--bc", default=None, help="canonical BC name (BC-side inbox mode)"
1091	    )
1092	    _read_inbox_mode.add_argument(
1093	        "--lead", default=None, help="canonical lead shop name (lead-side inbox mode)"
1094	    )
1095	    _add_removed_flag(read_inbox, "--bc-root")
1096	    read_inbox.add_argument(
1097	        "--work-id", required=True, help="work_id whose inbox message to read"
1098	    )
1099	
1100	    def _dispatch_read_inbox(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
1101	        if args.lead is not None:
1102	            return _cmd_read_lead_inbox(args)
1103	        return _cmd_read_inbox(args)
1104	
1105	    read_inbox.set_defaults(func=_dispatch_read_inbox)
1106	
1107	    pending = sub.add_parser(
1108	        "pending", help="enumerate pending mailbox entries (queries, not gates)"
1109	    )
1110	    pending_sub = pending.add_subparsers(dest="pending_target", required=True)
1111	
1112	    pending_inbox = pending_sub.add_parser(
1113	        "inbox",
1114	        help=(
1115	            "list inbox messages with no matching outbox response (BC side: --bc); "
1116	            "or list BC responses in the lead's inbox (lead side: --lead)"
1117	        ),
1118	    )
1119	    _pending_inbox_mode = pending_inbox.add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)
1120	    _pending_inbox_mode.add_argument(
1121	        "--bc", default=None, help="canonical BC name (BC-side inbox mode)"
1122	    )
1123	    _pending_inbox_mode.add_argument(
1124	        "--lead", default=None, help="canonical lead shop name (lead-side inbox mode)"
1125	    )
1126	    _add_removed_flag(pending_inbox, "--bc-root")
1127	
1128	    def _dispatch_pending_inbox(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
1129	        if args.lead is not None:
1130	            return _cmd_pending_lead_inbox(args)
1131	        return _cmd_pending_inbox(args)
1132	
1133	    pending_inbox.set_defaults(func=_dispatch_pending_inbox)
1134	
1135	    pending_outbox = pending_sub.add_parser(
1136	        "outbox",
1137	        help="list pending outbox responses across sibling BC clones (lead side)",
1138	    )
1139	    pending_outbox.add_argument(
1140	        "--lead",
1141	        required=True,
1142	        help="canonical lead shop name (resolved via registry to lead root path)",
1143	    )
1144	    _add_removed_flag(pending_outbox, "--lead-root")
1145	    pending_outbox.add_argument(
1146	        "--bc-name",
1147	        default=None,
1148	        dest="bc_name",
1149	        help="restrict to a single BC by canonical name",
1150	    )
1151	    pending_outbox.set_defaults(func=_cmd_pending_outbox)
1152	
1153	    consume = sub.add_parser(
1154	        "consume",
1155	        help="mark a mailbox entry as consumed (lead side)",
1156	    )
1157	    consume_sub = consume.add_subparsers(dest="consume_target", required=True)
1158	
1159	    consume_outbox = consume_sub.add_parser(
1160	        "outbox",
1161	        help=(
1162	            "mark a specific outbox row as consumed so it no longer appears "
1163	            "in 'pending outbox' output"
1164	        ),
1165	    )
1166	    consume_outbox.add_argument(
1167	        "--bc",
1168	        required=True,
1169	        help="canonical BC name (resolved via registry)",
1170	    )
1171	    _add_removed_flag(consume_outbox, "--bc-root")
1172	    consume_outbox.add_argument(
1173	        "--work-id",
1174	        required=True,
1175	        help="work_id of the outbox row to consume",
1176	    )
1177	    consume_outbox.add_argument(
1178	        "--message-type",
1179	        required=True,
1180	        choices=["clarify", "work_done", "mechanism_observation"],
1181	        help="message_type of the outbox row to consume",
1182	    )
1183	    consume_outbox.set_defaults(func=_cmd_consume_outbox)
1184	
1185	    prime = sub.add_parser(
1186	        "prime",
1187	        help="session-start orientation: DSN, DB health, pending inbox, and CLI reminder",
1188	    )
1189	    _prime_mode = prime.add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)
1190	    _prime_mode.add_argument("--bc", default=None, help="canonical BC name (resolved via registry)")
1191	    _prime_mode.add_argument("--lead", default=None, help="canonical lead shop name (lead mode)")
1192	    _add_removed_flag(prime, "--bc-root")
1193	    prime.set_defaults(func=_cmd_prime_dispatch)
1194	
1195	    dump = sub.add_parser(
1196	        "dump",
1197	        help="operator debugging: dump messages table rows to stdout as YAML",
1198	    )
1199	    dump.add_argument("--bc", default=None, help="restrict to this BC (canonical name)")
1200	    _add_removed_flag(dump, "--bc-root")
1201	    dump.add_argument(
1202	        "--direction",
1203	        default=None,
1204	        choices=["inbox", "outbox"],
1205	        help="restrict to inbox or outbox rows",
1206	    )
1207	    dump.add_argument(
1208	        "--limit",
1209	        type=int,
1210	        default=100,
1211	        help="maximum number of rows to return (default 100)",
1212	    )
1213	    dump.set_defaults(func=_cmd_dump)
1214	
1215	    watch = sub.add_parser(
1216	        "watch",
1217	        help=(
1218	            "Monitor-compatible watcher: drain pending messages then "
1219	            "LISTEN for new ones, printing one '<work_id> <message_type>' "
1220	            "line per event to stdout. Never exits unless the DB is unreachable. "
1221	            "Use --bc <name> for inbox watching (BC mode) or --lead <name> for "
1222	            "outbox watching across all BCs (lead mode)."
1223	        ),
1224	    )
1225	    _watch_mode = watch.add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)
1226	    _watch_mode.add_argument("--bc", default=None, help="canonical BC name (inbox watch mode)")
1227	    _watch_mode.add_argument(
1228	        "--lead",
1229	        default=None,
1230	        help="canonical lead shop name (outbox watch mode)",
1231	    )
1232	    # Register removed flags on watch but they cannot be in the mutex group;
1233	    # they're handled via the action which exits immediately.
1234	    _add_removed_flag(watch, "--bc-root")
1235	    _add_removed_flag(watch, "--lead-root")
1236	    watch.set_defaults(func=_cmd_watch)
1237	
1238	    # Registry subcommand
1239	    registry = sub.add_parser(
1240	        "registry",
1241	        help="manage the shop name registry (name-based addressing)",
1242	    )
1243	    registry_sub = registry.add_subparsers(dest="registry_cmd", required=True)
1244	
1245	    registry_add_cmd = registry_sub.add_parser(
1246	        "add",
1247	        help="register a shop by canonical name",
1248	    )
1249	    registry_add_cmd.add_argument("name", help="canonical shop name")
1250	    registry_add_cmd.add_argument(
1251	        "shop_root",
1252	        help="filesystem path to the shop root directory",
1253	    )
1254	    registry_add_cmd.add_argument(
1255	        "--lead-shop",
1256	        action="store_true",
1257	        default=False,
1258	        help="register as a lead shop (shop_type=lead) instead of a BC",
1259	    )
1260	    registry_add_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_registry_add)
1261	
1262	    registry_remove_cmd = registry_sub.add_parser(
1263	        "remove",
1264	        help="remove a shop from the registry by canonical name",
1265	    )
1266	    registry_remove_cmd.add_argument("name", help="canonical shop name to remove")
1267	    registry_remove_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_registry_remove)
1268	
1269	    registry_list_cmd = registry_sub.add_parser(
1270	        "list",
1271	        help="list all registered shops",
1272	    )
1273	    registry_list_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_registry_list)
1274	
1275	    registry_sync_cmd = registry_sub.add_parser(
1276	        "sync",
1277	        help="synchronise the registry from a BC manifest file",
1278	    )
1279	    registry_sync_cmd.add_argument(
1280	        "manifest",
1281	        help="path to YAML/JSON manifest with 'bcs' mapping of name -> root path",
1282	    )
1283	    registry_sync_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_registry_sync)
1284	
1285	    return parser
1286	
1287	
1288	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
1289	    parser = build_parser()
1290	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
1291	    return args.func(args)
1292	
1293	
1294	if __name__ == "__main__":
1295	    sys.exit(main())
1296
```
