 `./repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher/src/bc_launcher/manifest.py`

```text
1	"""
2	Manifest loading, validation, and sync logic for bc-container manifest commands.
3	
4	The manifest is a YAML file (bc-manifest.yaml) at the lead repo root. Its structure:
5	
6	  bcs:
7	    - name: shopsystem-messaging
8	      remote: https://github.com/dstengle/shopsystem-messaging.git
9	      role: bc
10	    - ...
11	
12	Required fields per entry: name, remote, role.
13	"""
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import re
17	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
18	from pathlib import Path
19	from typing import Protocol
20	
21	import yaml
22	
23	
24	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
25	# Data model
26	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
27	
28	REQUIRED_FIELDS = ("name", "remote", "role")
29	
30	GITHUB_URL_RE = re.compile(
31	    r"^(https://github\.com/[\w.\-]+/[\w.\-]+(\.git)?|git@github\.com:[\w.\-]+/[\w.\-]+(\.git)?)$"
32	)
33	
34	BC_NAME_RE = re.compile(r"^shopsystem-[a-z][a-z0-9\-]+$")
35	
36	
37	@dataclass
38	class BcEntry:
39	    name: str
40	    remote: str
41	    role: str
42	
43	
44	@dataclass
45	class Manifest:
46	    entries: list[BcEntry] = field(default_factory=list)
47	
48	
49	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
50	# Loading
51	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
52	
53	def load_manifest(path: Path) -> Manifest:
54	    """Parse bc-manifest.yaml and return a Manifest object.
55	
56	    Raises yaml.YAMLError on parse failure.
57	    Raises ValueError if the top-level structure is not a dict with a 'bcs' key.
58	    """
59	    text = path.read_text()
60	    data = yaml.safe_load(text)
61	    if not isinstance(data, dict) or "bcs" not in data:
62	        raise ValueError("Manifest must be a YAML mapping with a 'bcs' key")
63	    entries: list[BcEntry] = []
64	    for item in (data.get("bcs") or []):
65	        entry = BcEntry(
66	            name=item.get("name", ""),
67	            remote=item.get("remote", ""),
68	            role=item.get("role", ""),
69	        )
70	        entries.append(entry)
71	    return Manifest(entries=entries)
72	
73	
74	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
75	# Validation result
76	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
77	
78	@dataclass
79	class ValidationResult:
80	    ok: bool
81	    messages: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
82	    validated: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)   # names of BCs that passed
83	    failed: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)      # names of BCs that failed
84	
85	
86	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
87	# GitHub driver protocol
88	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
89	
90	class GitHubDriver(Protocol):
91	    """Check whether a GitHub remote URL is reachable."""
92	
93	    def is_reachable(self, url: str) -> bool:
94	        """Return True if the remote URL is reachable (repository exists)."""
95	        ...
96	
97	
98	class RealGitHubDriver:
99	    """Production GitHubDriver using git ls-remote."""
100	
101	    def is_reachable(self, url: str) -> bool:
102	        import subprocess
103	        result = subprocess.run(
104	            ["git", "ls-remote", "--exit-code", url],
105	            capture_output=True, timeout=15,
106	        )
107	        return result.returncode == 0
108	
109	
110	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
111	# Git driver protocol (for sync clones)
112	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
113	
114	class GitDriver(Protocol):
115	    """Minimal git operations for sync."""
116	
117	    def clone(self, remote_url: str, dest: Path) -> None:
118	        """Clone remote_url into dest directory."""
119	        ...
120	
121	    def get_remote_url(self, repo_path: Path) -> str | None:
122	        """Return the 'origin' remote URL of the repo at repo_path, or None."""
123	        ...
124	
125	
126	class RealGitDriver:
127	    """Production GitDriver using subprocess git."""
128	
129	    def clone(self, remote_url: str, dest: Path) -> None:
130	        import subprocess
131	        subprocess.run(
132	            ["git", "clone", remote_url, str(dest)],
133	            check=True,
134	        )
135	
136	    def get_remote_url(self, repo_path: Path) -> str | None:
137	        import subprocess
138	        result = subprocess.run(
139	            ["git", "-C", str(repo_path), "remote", "get-url", "origin"],
140	            capture_output=True, text=True,
141	        )
142	        if result.returncode == 0:
143	            return result.stdout.strip()
144	        return None
145	
146	
147	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
148	# ManifestController
149	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
150	
151	class ManifestController:
152	    """
153	    Implements bc-container manifest validate / list / sync.
154	
155	    Accepts GitHubDriver and GitDriver at construction for testability.
156	    """
157	
158	    def __init__(
159	        self,
160	        github_driver: GitHubDriver | None = None,
161	        git_driver: GitDriver | None = None,
162	    ) -> None:
163	        self._github = github_driver or RealGitHubDriver()
164	        self._git = git_driver or RealGitDriver()
165	
166	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
167	    # validate
168	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
169	
170	    def validate(
171	        self,
172	        manifest_path: Path,
173	        repos_dir: Path | None = None,
174	    ) -> ValidationResult:
175	        """
176	        Validate the manifest at manifest_path.
177	
178	        Checks:
179	        - YAML parseable
180	        - required fields present on each entry
181	        - canonical name pattern
182	        - GitHub remote URL format and reachability
183	        - repos_dir consistency (missing clones, extra dirs, remote mismatches)
184	        """
185	        result = ValidationResult(ok=True)
186	
187	        # 1. Parse YAML
188	        try:
189	            manifest = load_manifest(manifest_path)
190	        except yaml.YAMLError as exc:
191	            result.ok = False
192	            result.messages.append(f"YAML parse error: {exc}")
193	            return result
194	        except ValueError as exc:
195	            result.ok = False
196	            result.messages.append(str(exc))
197	            return result
198	
199	        result.messages.append("Manifest is syntactically valid")
200	
201	        # 2. Per-entry checks
202	        entry_ok = True
203	        for entry in manifest.entries:
204	            entry_failed = False
205	
206	            # Required fields
207	            for field_name in REQUIRED_FIELDS:
208	                if not getattr(entry, field_name):
209	                    result.ok = False
210	                    entry_failed = True
211	                    result.messages.append(
212	                        f"Entry '{entry.name}': missing required field '{field_name}'"
213	                    )
214	
215	            # GitHub URL format
216	            if entry.remote and not GITHUB_URL_RE.match(entry.remote):
217	                result.ok = False
218	                entry_failed = True
219	                result.messages.append(
220	                    f"Entry '{entry.name}': remote URL '{entry.remote}' is not a valid GitHub URL"
221	                )
222	
223	            # GitHub reachability (only if URL format is valid)
224	            if entry.remote and GITHUB_URL_RE.match(entry.remote):
225	                if not self._github.is_reachable(entry.remote):
226	                    result.ok = False
227	                    entry_failed = True
228	                    result.messages.append(
229	                        f"Entry '{entry.name}': remote URL '{entry.remote}' is unreachable "
230	                        f"(repository not found or connection refused)"
231	                    )
232	
233	            if not entry_failed:
234	                result.validated.append(entry.name)
235	                result.messages.append(f"Entry '{entry.name}': valid")
236	            else:
237	                result.failed.append(entry.name)
238	
239	        # 3. repos_dir consistency checks
240	        if repos_dir is not None and repos_dir.exists():
241	            declared_names = {e.name for e in manifest.entries}
242	            actual_dirs = {d.name for d in repos_dir.iterdir() if d.is_dir()}
243	
244	            # Extra dirs not in manifest
245	            extra = actual_dirs - declared_names
246	            for name in sorted(extra):
247	                result.ok = False
248	                result.messages.append(
249	                    f"Repos directory contains unexpected entry '{name}' not in manifest"
250	                )
251	
252	            # Missing clones
253	            for entry in manifest.entries:
254	                clone_path = repos_dir / entry.name
255	                if not clone_path.exists():
256	                    result.ok = False
257	                    result.messages.append(
258	                        f"Entry '{entry.name}': declared in manifest but no local clone found"
259	                    )
260	                elif clone_path.is_dir():
261	                    # Check remote mismatch
262	                    actual_url = self._git.get_remote_url(clone_path)
263	                    if actual_url is not None and actual_url != entry.remote:
264	                        result.ok = False
265	                        result.messages.append(
266	                            f"Entry '{entry.name}': remote URL mismatch — "
267	                            f"manifest declares '{entry.remote}' but clone has '{actual_url}'"
268	                        )
269	
270	        return result
271	
272	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
273	    # list
274	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
275	
276	    def list_bcs(self, manifest_path: Path) -> tuple[int, str]:
277	        """
278	        Return (exit_code, output) listing one BC per line.
279	
280	        Format per line: <canonical-name> <remote-url>
281	        Exits zero if manifest is valid; non-zero on parse error.
282	        """
283	        try:
284	            manifest = load_manifest(manifest_path)
285	        except (yaml.YAMLError, ValueError) as exc:
286	            return 1, f"Error loading manifest: {exc}\n"
287	
288	        lines = [f"{entry.name} {entry.remote}" for entry in manifest.entries]
289	        return 0, "\n".join(lines) + ("\n" if lines else "")
290	
291	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
292	    # sync
293	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
294	
295	    def sync(
296	        self,
297	        manifest_path: Path,
298	        repos_dir: Path,
299	    ) -> tuple[int, str]:
300	        """
301	        Sync repos_dir against the manifest.
302	
303	        - Clone missing repos.
304	        - Skip existing repos with matching remote.
305	        - Warn about repos_dir entries not in manifest (but do not delete them).
306	
307	        Returns (exit_code, output).
308	        """
309	        try:
310	            manifest = load_manifest(manifest_path)
311	        except (yaml.YAMLError, ValueError) as exc:
312	            return 1, f"Error loading manifest: {exc}\n"
313	
314	        repos_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
315	        declared_names = {e.name for e in manifest.entries}
316	
317	        lines: list[str] = []
318	        clone_count = 0
319	
320	        for entry in manifest.entries:
321	            dest = repos_dir / entry.name
322	            if dest.exists() and dest.is_dir():
323	                actual_url = self._git.get_remote_url(dest)
324	                if actual_url == entry.remote:
325	                    lines.append(
326	                        f"Skipped '{entry.name}': already present with matching remote"
327	                    )
328	                else:
329	                    lines.append(
330	                        f"Skipped '{entry.name}': already present (remote mismatch — "
331	                        f"expected '{entry.remote}', found '{actual_url}')"
332	                    )
333	            else:
334	                self._git.clone(entry.remote, dest)
335	                clone_count += 1
336	                lines.append(f"Cloned '{entry.name}' from '{entry.remote}'")
337	
338	        # Warn about extra dirs
339	        if repos_dir.exists():
340	            actual_dirs = {d.name for d in repos_dir.iterdir() if d.is_dir()}
341	            extra = actual_dirs - declared_names
342	            for name in sorted(extra):
343	                lines.append(f"Warning: '{name}' is present in repos dir but not declared in manifest")
344	
345	        return 0, "\n".join(lines) + ("\n" if lines else "")
346
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher/src/bc_launcher/cli.py`

```text
1	"""
2	bc-container CLI entry point.
3	
4	Subcommands: launch, attach, inject, monitor, stop, status, list, manifest
5	"""
6	from __future__ import annotations
7	
8	import argparse
9	import sys
10	from pathlib import Path
11	
12	from bc_launcher.controller import BcContainerController
13	from bc_launcher.driver import RealDockerDriver
14	from bc_launcher.manifest import ManifestController, RealGitDriver, RealGitHubDriver
15	
16	
17	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
18	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
19	        prog="bc-container",
20	        description="Manage BC Docker containers for the shopsystem framework.",
21	    )
22	    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="subcommand", required=True)
23	
24	    # launch
25	    p_launch = sub.add_parser("launch", help="Start a BC container")
26	    p_launch.add_argument("bc_name", help="BC name (e.g. shopsystem-messaging)")
27	    p_launch.add_argument("--repo-url", help="Git repo URL to clone inside the container")
28	    p_launch.add_argument("--shopmsg-dsn", help="SHOPMSG_DSN value for the container")
29	    p_launch.add_argument("--network", help="Docker network to attach")
30	    p_launch.add_argument("--startup-prompt", help="Text to inject into tmux after start")
31	
32	    # attach
33	    p_attach = sub.add_parser("attach", help="Attach to the BC container tmux session")
34	    p_attach.add_argument("bc_name", help="BC name")
35	
36	    # inject
37	    p_inject = sub.add_parser("inject", help="Send text to the BC container tmux session")
38	    p_inject.add_argument("bc_name", help="BC name")
39	    p_inject.add_argument("prompt", help="Text to send")
40	
41	    # monitor
42	    p_monitor = sub.add_parser("monitor", help="Stream BC container tmux output")
43	    p_monitor.add_argument("bc_name", help="BC name")
44	
45	    # stop
46	    p_stop = sub.add_parser("stop", help="Stop and remove the BC container")
47	    p_stop.add_argument("bc_name", help="BC name")
48	
49	    # status
50	    p_status = sub.add_parser("status", help="Report BC container state")
51	    p_status.add_argument("bc_name", help="BC name")
52	
53	    # list
54	    sub.add_parser("list", help="List all known BC containers")
55	
56	    # manifest
57	    p_manifest = sub.add_parser("manifest", help="Manage the BC manifest file")
58	    manifest_sub = p_manifest.add_subparsers(dest="manifest_subcommand", required=True)
59	
60	    # manifest validate
61	    p_mv = manifest_sub.add_parser("validate", help="Validate the BC manifest")
62	    p_mv.add_argument(
63	        "--manifest", default="bc-manifest.yaml",
64	        help="Path to the manifest file (default: bc-manifest.yaml)",
65	    )
66	    p_mv.add_argument(
67	        "--repos-dir", default=None,
68	        help="Path to the repos directory for consistency checks",
69	    )
70	
71	    # manifest list
72	    p_ml = manifest_sub.add_parser("list", help="List BCs declared in the manifest")
73	    p_ml.add_argument(
74	        "--manifest", default="bc-manifest.yaml",
75	        help="Path to the manifest file (default: bc-manifest.yaml)",
76	    )
77	
78	    # manifest sync
79	    p_ms = manifest_sub.add_parser("sync", help="Sync repos directory from the manifest")
80	    p_ms.add_argument(
81	        "--manifest", default="bc-manifest.yaml",
82	        help="Path to the manifest file (default: bc-manifest.yaml)",
83	    )
84	    p_ms.add_argument(
85	        "--repos-dir", default="repos",
86	        help="Path to the repos directory (default: repos)",
87	    )
88	
89	    return parser
90	
91	
92	def _run_manifest(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
93	    """Handle 'bc-container manifest <subcommand>'."""
94	    mc = ManifestController(
95	        github_driver=RealGitHubDriver(),
96	        git_driver=RealGitDriver(),
97	    )
98	    manifest_path = Path(args.manifest)
99	
100	    if args.manifest_subcommand == "validate":
101	        repos_dir = Path(args.repos_dir) if args.repos_dir else None
102	        result = mc.validate(manifest_path, repos_dir=repos_dir)
103	        for msg in result.messages:
104	            sys.stdout.write(msg + "\n")
105	        return 0 if result.ok else 1
106	
107	    elif args.manifest_subcommand == "list":
108	        exit_code, output = mc.list_bcs(manifest_path)
109	        sys.stdout.write(output)
110	        return exit_code
111	
112	    elif args.manifest_subcommand == "sync":
113	        repos_dir = Path(args.repos_dir)
114	        exit_code, output = mc.sync(manifest_path, repos_dir)
115	        sys.stdout.write(output)
116	        return exit_code
117	
118	    return 0
119	
120	
121	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
122	    parser = build_parser()
123	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
124	
125	    if args.subcommand == "manifest":
126	        return _run_manifest(args)
127	
128	    controller = BcContainerController(RealDockerDriver())
129	
130	    if args.subcommand == "launch":
131	        result = controller.launch(
132	            bc_name=args.bc_name,
133	            repo_url=getattr(args, "repo_url", None),
134	            shopmsg_dsn=getattr(args, "shopmsg_dsn", None),
135	            startup_prompt=getattr(args, "startup_prompt", None),
136	            network=getattr(args, "network", None),
137	        )
138	        sys.stdout.write(result.stdout)
139	        if result.stderr:
140	            sys.stderr.write(result.stderr)
141	        return result.exit_code
142	
143	    elif args.subcommand == "attach":
144	        # attach replaces the process — no return value
145	        controller.attach(args.bc_name)
146	        return 0  # unreachable in production
147	
148	    elif args.subcommand == "inject":
149	        result = controller.inject(args.bc_name, args.prompt)
150	        sys.stdout.write(result.stdout)
151	        if result.stderr:
152	            sys.stderr.write(result.stderr)
153	        return result.exit_code
154	
155	    elif args.subcommand == "monitor":
156	        result = controller.monitor(args.bc_name)
157	        sys.stdout.write(result.stdout)
158	        if result.stderr:
159	            sys.stderr.write(result.stderr)
160	        return result.exit_code
161	
162	    elif args.subcommand == "stop":
163	        result = controller.stop(args.bc_name)
164	        sys.stdout.write(result.stdout)
165	        if result.stderr:
166	            sys.stderr.write(result.stderr)
167	        return result.exit_code
168	
169	    elif args.subcommand == "status":
170	        result = controller.status(args.bc_name)
171	        sys.stdout.write(result.stdout)
172	        if result.stderr:
173	            sys.stderr.write(result.stderr)
174	        return result.exit_code
175	
176	    elif args.subcommand == "list":
177	        result = controller.list_containers()
178	        sys.stdout.write(result.stdout)
179	        if result.stderr:
180	            sys.stderr.write(result.stderr)
181	        return result.exit_code
182	
183	    return 0
184	
185	
186	if __name__ == "__main__":
187	    sys.exit(main())
188
```

 `./repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher/src/bc_launcher/controller.py`

```text
1	"""
2	Business logic for bc-container subcommands.
3	
4	All Docker interaction goes through the DockerDriver interface, making this
5	layer fully testable without a live Docker daemon.
6	"""
7	from __future__ import annotations
8	
9	import os
10	import sys
11	from dataclasses import dataclass
12	
13	from bc_launcher.driver import ContainerMount, DockerDriver
14	
15	
16	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
17	# Constants / defaults
18	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
19	
20	CONTAINER_WORKSPACE = "/workspace"
21	AGENT_TMUX_SESSION = "agent"
22	BC_IMAGE = "ghcr.io/shopsystem/bc-base:latest"
23	SHOPMSG_DSN_ENV = "SHOPMSG_DSN"
24	
25	
26	def _container_name(bc_name: str) -> str:
27	    return f"bc-{bc_name}"
28	
29	
30	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
31	# Result type returned by commands that produce output
32	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
33	
34	@dataclass
35	class CommandResult:
36	    exit_code: int
37	    stdout: str
38	    stderr: str = ""
39	
40	
41	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
42	# Subcommand implementations
43	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
44	
45	class BcContainerController:
46	    """
47	    Pure-Python controller for bc-container operations.
48	
49	    Accepts a DockerDriver at construction time so tests can inject fakes.
50	    """
51	
52	    def __init__(self, driver: DockerDriver) -> None:
53	        self._driver = driver
54	
55	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
56	    # launch
57	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
58	
59	    def launch(
60	        self,
61	        bc_name: str,
62	        repo_url: str | None = None,
63	        shopmsg_dsn: str | None = None,
64	        startup_prompt: str | None = None,
65	        network: str | None = None,
66	    ) -> CommandResult:
67	        """
68	        Start a Docker container for the named BC.
69	
70	        Idempotent: if the container is already running, report and exit 0.
71	        """
72	        container = _container_name(bc_name)
73	
74	        if self._driver.is_running(container):
75	            return CommandResult(
76	                exit_code=0,
77	                stdout=f"{container} is already running\n",
78	            )
79	
80	        # Build environment
81	        env: dict[str, str] = {}
82	        if shopmsg_dsn:
83	            env[SHOPMSG_DSN_ENV] = shopmsg_dsn
84	        elif dsn := os.environ.get(SHOPMSG_DSN_ENV):
85	            env[SHOPMSG_DSN_ENV] = dsn
86	
87	        # Mounts: only the BC's own workspace mount + optional SHOPMSG socket
88	        mounts: list[tuple[str, str, str]] = []
89	
90	        # SHOPMSG_DSN may be a postgres DSN (no socket mount needed) or a
91	        # unix socket path.  If the DSN value looks like a socket file, add a
92	        # bind mount for it.
93	        dsn_value = env.get(SHOPMSG_DSN_ENV, "")
94	        if dsn_value.startswith("/") and not dsn_value.startswith("//"):
95	            # It's a host socket path — mount the containing directory
96	            socket_dir = os.path.dirname(dsn_value)
97	            mounts.append(("bind", socket_dir, socket_dir))
98	
99	        self._driver.run(
100	            container_name=container,
101	            image=BC_IMAGE,
102	            env=env,
103	            mounts=mounts,
104	            network=network,
105	            detach=True,
106	        )
107	
108	        out_lines: list[str] = [f"Started container {container}\n"]
109	
110	        # Clone repository if URL provided
111	        if repo_url:
112	            clone_result = self._driver.exec_run(
113	                container,
114	                ["git", "clone", repo_url, CONTAINER_WORKSPACE],
115	            )
116	            if clone_result.returncode != 0:
117	                return CommandResult(
118	                    exit_code=1,
119	                    stdout="".join(out_lines),
120	                    stderr=f"git clone failed: {clone_result.stderr}",
121	                )
122	            out_lines.append(f"Cloned {repo_url} into {CONTAINER_WORKSPACE}\n")
123	
124	            # bd dolt pull
125	            bd_result = self._driver.exec_run(
126	                container,
127	                ["bd", "dolt", "pull"],
128	            )
129	            out_lines.append("Ran bd dolt pull\n")
130	
131	        # Start tmux session
132	        tmux_result = self._driver.exec_run(
133	            container,
134	            ["tmux", "new-session", "-d", "-s", AGENT_TMUX_SESSION],
135	        )
136	        out_lines.append(f"Started tmux session '{AGENT_TMUX_SESSION}'\n")
137	
138	        # Optional startup prompt
139	        if startup_prompt:
140	            self._driver.exec_run(
141	                container,
142	                ["tmux", "send-keys", "-t", AGENT_TMUX_SESSION,
143	                 startup_prompt, "Enter"],
144	            )
145	            out_lines.append(f"Injected startup prompt: {startup_prompt!r}\n")
146	
147	        return CommandResult(exit_code=0, stdout="".join(out_lines))
148	
149	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
150	    # attach
151	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
152	
153	    def attach(self, bc_name: str) -> None:
154	        """
155	        Attach to the BC container's tmux session interactively.
156	        Replaces the current process via exec.
157	        """
158	        container = _container_name(bc_name)
159	        self._driver.exec_interactive(
160	            container,
161	            ["tmux", "attach-session", "-t", AGENT_TMUX_SESSION],
162	        )
163	
164	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
165	    # inject
166	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
167	
168	    def inject(self, bc_name: str, prompt_text: str) -> CommandResult:
169	        """Send text to the container's tmux session."""
170	        container = _container_name(bc_name)
171	        self._driver.exec_run(
172	            container,
173	            ["tmux", "send-keys", "-t", AGENT_TMUX_SESSION, prompt_text, "Enter"],
174	        )
175	        return CommandResult(
176	            exit_code=0,
177	            stdout=f"Sent {prompt_text!r} to {AGENT_TMUX_SESSION} in {container}\n",
178	        )
179	
180	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
181	    # monitor
182	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
183	
184	    def monitor(self, bc_name: str) -> CommandResult:
185	        """Capture and return the current contents of the tmux session pane."""
186	        container = _container_name(bc_name)
187	        result = self._driver.exec_run(
188	            container,
189	            ["tmux", "capture-pane", "-p", "-t", AGENT_TMUX_SESSION],
190	        )
191	        return CommandResult(
192	            exit_code=result.returncode,
193	            stdout=result.stdout,
194	            stderr=result.stderr,
195	        )
196	
197	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
198	    # stop
199	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
200	
201	    def stop(self, bc_name: str) -> CommandResult:
202	        """Stop and remove the BC container."""
203	        container = _container_name(bc_name)
204	        self._driver.stop(container)
205	        return CommandResult(exit_code=0, stdout=f"Stopped {container}\n")
206	
207	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
208	    # status
209	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
210	
211	    def status(self, bc_name: str) -> CommandResult:
212	        """Report running state of the BC container and its tmux session."""
213	        container = _container_name(bc_name)
214	        is_running = self._driver.is_running(container)
215	
216	        if not is_running:
217	            return CommandResult(
218	                exit_code=0,
219	                stdout=(
220	                    f"bc_name: {bc_name}\n"
221	                    f"container: {container}\n"
222	                    f"container_state: stopped\n"
223	                ),
224	            )
225	
226	        # Check tmux session
227	        tmux_result = self._driver.exec_run(
228	            container,
229	            ["tmux", "has-session", "-t", AGENT_TMUX_SESSION],
230	        )
231	        tmux_state = "active" if tmux_result.returncode == 0 else "inactive"
232	
233	        return CommandResult(
234	            exit_code=0,
235	            stdout=(
236	                f"bc_name: {bc_name}\n"
237	                f"container: {container}\n"
238	                f"container_state: running\n"
239	                f"tmux_session: {tmux_state}\n"
240	            ),
241	        )
242	
243	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
244	    # list
245	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
246	
247	    def list_containers(self) -> CommandResult:
248	        """List all known BC containers with their states."""
249	        infos = self._driver.list_bc_containers()
250	        if not infos:
251	            return CommandResult(exit_code=0, stdout="No BC containers found.\n")
252	
253	        lines: list[str] = []
254	        for info in infos:
255	            # Derive bc_name by stripping leading "bc-"
256	            bc_name = info.name.removeprefix("bc-")
257	            state = "running" if info.running else "stopped"
258	            lines.append(f"{bc_name}: {state}\n")
259	
260	        return CommandResult(exit_code=0, stdout="".join(lines))
261	
262	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
263	    # isolation check (used by tests and the isolate-check subcommand)
264	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
265	
266	    def get_bind_mounts(self, container_name: str) -> list[ContainerMount]:
267	        """Return only bind-type mounts for a running container."""
268	        all_mounts = self._driver.get_mounts(container_name)
269	        return [m for m in all_mounts if m.type == "bind"]
270	
271	    def last_command(self) -> list[str]:
272	        return self._driver.last_command()
273
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
"""
Docker driver interface and real implementation.

The DockerDriver protocol is the seam between bc_launcher business logic and
the real Docker daemon.  Tests inject a FakeDockerDriver; production code uses
RealDockerDriver which shells out to the docker CLI.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import subprocess
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Protocol


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Data types
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@dataclass
class ContainerMount:
    """Represents one mount entry for a running container."""
    type: str          # "bind", "volume", "tmpfs", …
    source: str        # host-side source path / volume name / socket path
    destination: str   # path inside the container


@dataclass
class ContainerInfo:
    """Aggregated state for a BC container."""
    name: str
    running: bool
    mounts: list[ContainerMount] = field(default_factory=list)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Protocol
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class DockerDriver(Protocol):
    """Minimal Docker operations needed by bc_launcher."""

    def is_running(self, container_name: str) -> bool:
        """Return True if the named container is currently running."""
        ...

    def run(
        self,
        container_name: str,
        image: str,
        env: dict[str, str],
        mounts: list[tuple[str, str, str]],  # (type, source, dest)
        network: str | None,
        detach: bool,
    ) -> None:
        """Start a new container."""
        ...

    def exec_run(self, container_name: str, command: list[str]) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:
        """Execute a command inside a running container."""
        ...

    def exec_interactive(self, container_name: str, command: list[str]) -> None:
        """Execute an interactive command inside a running container (replaces process)."""
        ...

    def stop(self, container_name: str) -> None:
        """Stop and remove the named container."""
        ...

    def list_bc_containers(self) -> list[ContainerInfo]:
        """Return ContainerInfo for every container whose name starts with 'bc-'."""
        ...

    def get_mounts(self, container_name: str) -> list[ContainerMount]:
        """Return the mount list for a running container."""
        ...

    def last_command(self) -> list[str]:
        """Return the most recently built shell command (for test assertions)."""
        ...
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-22 16:38:46Z)**_

All four files written. Here is the report.

---

**Committed answers to the four questions**

1. **Where does the product name come from?** A new top-level `product` field in `bc-manifest.yaml`. This is the only file `bc-container launch` already reads before starting a container; it carries the authoritative identity of the deployment unit. A CLI flag would require every caller to know the name; a registry lookup adds an out-of-band dependency. The manifest field is the minimal, co-located answer.

2. **What is the derivation rule?** Slugify: lowercase the value, replace every run of whitespace with a single hyphen. "shopsystem product" → `shopsystem-product`; "My Ecommerce Shop" → `my-ecommerce-shop`. No other transformations. This produces valid Docker network names and mirrors the existing BC naming convention.

3. **Is `--network` a default or a replacement?** The derived name is the DEFAULT when `--network` is omitted. `--network` is a full override: when provided, it is used as-is and no automatic network creation is performed. When omitted and `bc-manifest.yaml` has no `product` field (or no manifest exists), the command exits non-zero with a diagnostic.

4. **Does `bc-container launch` create the network?** Yes. Before calling `docker run`, `launch` checks whether the network exists and issues `docker network create <name>` if it does not. If the network already exists, creation is skipped silently. An explicit `--network` suppresses this behavior entirely.

---

**Scenario titles and sufficiency**

| File | Scenario | Concrete Given | Measurable Then | One behavior |
|------|----------|----------------|-----------------|--------------|
| `01-derive.gherkin` | Derives network from `product` field | manifest with `product: shopsystem product` | `--network shopsystem-product` in recorded docker run flags | yes |
| `01-derive.gherkin` | Slugifies spaces and uppercasing | `product: My Ecommerce Shop` | `--network my-ecommerce-shop` in flags | yes |
| `01-derive.gherkin` | Exits non-zero with no manifest and no flag | no manifest, no `--network` | non-zero exit + specific stderr text | yes |
| `02-create.gherkin` | Creates network before container when absent | network does not exist | `docker network create` called before `docker run` | yes |
| `02-create.gherkin` | Does not re-create existing network | network already exists | `docker network create` NOT called | yes |
| `03-override.gherkin` | `--network` flag uses given name, not derived | manifest has `product`, `--network custom-net` given | `--network custom-net` in flags, `shopsystem-product` absent | yes |
| `03-override.gherkin` | `--network` suppresses auto-create | network absent, `--network` provided | `docker network create` NOT called | yes |
| `04-shared.gherkin` | Two containers share derived network | two launches, same manifest | both docker run commands carry `--network shopsystem-product` | yes |
| `04-shared.gherkin` | Network create called only once across two launches | second container launch with network already present | `docker network create` recorded exactly once total | yes |

All nine scenarios pass the sufficiency check: Givens are concrete named values; Thens are assertions on the FakeDockerDriver's recorded command log (measurable without a live Docker daemon); each scenario exercises exactly one behavior.

**File paths for the Architect:**
- `/tmp/network-scenarios/01-derive.gherkin`
- `/tmp/network-scenarios/02-create.gherkin`
- `/tmp/network-scenarios/03-override.gherkin`
- `/tmp/network-scenarios/04-shared.gherkin`

PO committed to 9 scenarios across 4 files. Key design decisions:
- **Product name source:** new `product` field in `bc-manifest.yaml`
- **Derivation:** slugify — lowercase, spaces→hyphens
- **`--network`:** still overrides; derived name is the default; exits non-zero with no manifest and no flag
- **Network creation:** `bc-container launch` creates the network if absent (derived only, not explicit)

You are the Lead Architect for the shopsystem product lead shop. You need to dispatch `assign_scenarios` to `shopsystem-bc-launcher` for a new capability: product-scoped Docker network naming.

## PO-authored scenarios (9 scenarios, 4 files)

- `/tmp/network-scenarios/01-derive.gherkin` — 3 scenarios: derive network from `product` field, slugify spaces/uppercasing, exit non-zero with no manifest and no flag
- `/tmp/network-scenarios/02-create.gherkin` — 2 scenarios: create network before container when absent, skip create when network exists
- `/tmp/network-scenarios/03-override.gherkin` — 2 scenarios: `--network` uses given name not derived, `--network` suppresses auto-create
- `/tmp/network-scenarios/04-shared.gherkin` — 2 scenarios: two containers share derived network, network create called only once across two launches

## Design decisions from PO

- Product name from a new top-level `product` field in `bc-manifest.yaml`
- Derivation: slugify (lowercase, spaces→hyphens; e.g. "shopsystem product" → "shopsystem-product")
- `--network` flag overrides derived name; derived name is default when `--network` omitted; exits non-zero if no manifest and no `--network`
- `bc-container launch` creates the network (`docker network create`) if absent when using derived name; explicit `--network` suppresses auto-create

## Your job

1. **Verify BC pre-state empirically.** Confirm the capability does not exist:
   - Does `bc-manifest.yaml` schema have a `product` field? Check `repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher/src/bc_launcher/manifest.py`
   - Does `BcContainerController.launch()` derive a network name from any manifest field? Check `repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher/src/bc_launcher/controller.py`
   - Does `DockerDriver` have a `network_create` method? Check `repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher/src/bc_launcher/driver.py`
   Run `grep -n "product\|network_create\|slugify\|derive" repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher/src/bc_launcher/*.py` and cite what you find.

2. **Read each scenario file** and verify technical claims:
   - The `product` field name matches what manifest.py defines (or confirms is absent)
   - The slugify rule is unambiguous and implementable
   - The `FakeDockerDriver` reference is consistent with existing test patterns in the BC (check `repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher/tests/`)

3. **Check existing BC-side scenario hashes:**
   ```
   grep -r "@scenario_hash" /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher/features/*.feature
   ```
   Confirm no existing scenario covers network derivation.

4. **Create a beads issue:**

   ```
   bd create --title="assign_scenarios: product-scoped Docker network naming in bc-container launch" --description="New capability: bc-container launch derives Docker network name from product field in bc-manifest.yaml (slugified). No product field exists in manifest schema, no network derivation logic in controller, no network_create in driver — verified by grep. Dispatching 9 PO-authored scenarios across 4 files." --type=feature --priority=2
   ```

5. **Dispatch via `shop-msg send assign_scenarios`** to `shopsystem-bc-launcher` with all 4 scenario files via `--scenario-file`. Include any technical corrections to the scenarios in the dispatch description if found.

6. **Verify inbox:**

   ```
   /workspaces/shopsystem-product/.venv/bin/shop-msg read inbox --bc "shopsystem-bc-launcher" --work-id <id>
   ```

7. **Report** (under 250 words): empirical Q1 findings (what grep found or didn't find), scenario review notes, work_id, hashes, inbox verified.

```text
1	"""
2	Manifest loading, validation, and sync logic for bc-container manifest commands.
3	
4	The manifest is a YAML file (bc-manifest.yaml) at the lead repo root. Its structure:
5	
6	  bcs:
7	    - name: shopsystem-messaging
8	      remote: https://github.com/dstengle/shopsystem-messaging.git
9	      role: bc
10	    - ...
11	
12	Required fields per entry: name, remote, role.
13	"""
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import re
17	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
18	from pathlib import Path
19	from typing import Protocol
20	
21	import yaml
22	
23	
24	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
25	# Data model
26	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
27	
28	REQUIRED_FIELDS = ("name", "remote", "role")
29	
30	GITHUB_URL_RE = re.compile(
31	    r"^(https://github\.com/[\w.\-]+/[\w.\-]+(\.git)?|git@github\.com:[\w.\-]+/[\w.\-]+(\.git)?)$"
32	)
33	
34	BC_NAME_RE = re.compile(r"^shopsystem-[a-z][a-z0-9\-]+$")
35	
36	
37	@dataclass
38	class BcEntry:
39	    name: str
40	    remote: str
41	    role: str
42	
43	
44	@dataclass
45	class Manifest:
46	    entries: list[BcEntry] = field(default_factory=list)
47	
48	
49	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
50	# Loading
51	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
52	
53	def load_manifest(path: Path) -> Manifest:
54	    """Parse bc-manifest.yaml and return a Manifest object.
55	
56	    Raises yaml.YAMLError on parse failure.
57	    Raises ValueError if the top-level structure is not a dict with a 'bcs' key.
58	    """
59	    text = path.read_text()
60	    data = yaml.safe_load(text)
61	    if not isinstance(data, dict) or "bcs" not in data:
62	        raise ValueError("Manifest must be a YAML mapping with a 'bcs' key")
63	    entries: list[BcEntry] = []
64	    for item in (data.get("bcs") or []):
65	        entry = BcEntry(
66	            name=item.get("name", ""),
67	            remote=item.get("remote", ""),
68	            role=item.get("role", ""),
69	        )
70	        entries.append(entry)
71	    return Manifest(entries=entries)
72	
73	
74	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
75	# Validation result
76	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
77	
78	@dataclass
79	class ValidationResult:
80	    ok: bool
81	    messages: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
82	    validated: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)   # names of BCs that passed
83	    failed: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)      # names of BCs that failed
84	
85	
86	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
87	# GitHub driver protocol
88	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
89	
90	class GitHubDriver(Protocol):
91	    """Check whether a GitHub remote URL is reachable."""
92	
93	    def is_reachable(self, url: str) -> bool:
94	        """Return True if the remote URL is reachable (repository exists)."""
95	        ...
96	
97	
98	class RealGitHubDriver:
99	    """Production GitHubDriver using git ls-remote."""
100	
101	    def is_reachable(self, url: str) -> bool:
102	        import subprocess
103	        result = subprocess.run(
104	            ["git", "ls-remote", "--exit-code", url],
105	            capture_output=True, timeout=15,
106	        )
107	        return result.returncode == 0
108	
109	
110	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
111	# Git driver protocol (for sync clones)
112	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
113	
114	class GitDriver(Protocol):
115	    """Minimal git operations for sync."""
116	
117	    def clone(self, remote_url: str, dest: Path) -> None:
118	        """Clone remote_url into dest directory."""
119	        ...
120	
121	    def get_remote_url(self, repo_path: Path) -> str | None:
122	        """Return the 'origin' remote URL of the repo at repo_path, or None."""
123	        ...
124	
125	
126	class RealGitDriver:
127	    """Production GitDriver using subprocess git."""
128	
129	    def clone(self, remote_url: str, dest: Path) -> None:
130	        import subprocess
131	        subprocess.run(
132	            ["git", "clone", remote_url, str(dest)],
133	            check=True,
134	        )
135	
136	    def get_remote_url(self, repo_path: Path) -> str | None:
137	        import subprocess
138	        result = subprocess.run(
139	            ["git", "-C", str(repo_path), "remote", "get-url", "origin"],
140	            capture_output=True, text=True,
141	        )
142	        if result.returncode == 0:
143	            return result.stdout.strip()
144	        return None
145	
146	
147	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
148	# ManifestController
149	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
150	
151	class ManifestController:
152	    """
153	    Implements bc-container manifest validate / list / sync.
154	
155	    Accepts GitHubDriver and GitDriver at construction for testability.
156	    """
157	
158	    def __init__(
159	        self,
160	        github_driver: GitHubDriver | None = None,
161	        git_driver: GitDriver | None = None,
162	    ) -> None:
163	        self._github = github_driver or RealGitHubDriver()
164	        self._git = git_driver or RealGitDriver()
165	
166	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
167	    # validate
168	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
169	
170	    def validate(
171	        self,
172	        manifest_path: Path,
173	        repos_dir: Path | None = None,
174	    ) -> ValidationResult:
175	        """
176	        Validate the manifest at manifest_path.
177	
178	        Checks:
179	        - YAML parseable
180	        - required fields present on each entry
181	        - canonical name pattern
182	        - GitHub remote URL format and reachability
183	        - repos_dir consistency (missing clones, extra dirs, remote mismatches)
184	        """
185	        result = ValidationResult(ok=True)
186	
187	        # 1. Parse YAML
188	        try:
189	            manifest = load_manifest(manifest_path)
190	        except yaml.YAMLError as exc:
191	            result.ok = False
192	            result.messages.append(f"YAML parse error: {exc}")
193	            return result
194	        except ValueError as exc:
195	            result.ok = False
196	            result.messages.append(str(exc))
197	            return result
198	
199	        result.messages.append("Manifest is syntactically valid")
200	
201	        # 2. Per-entry checks
202	        entry_ok = True
203	        for entry in manifest.entries:
204	            entry_failed = False
205	
206	            # Required fields
207	            for field_name in REQUIRED_FIELDS:
208	                if not getattr(entry, field_name):
209	                    result.ok = False
210	                    entry_failed = True
211	                    result.messages.append(
212	                        f"Entry '{entry.name}': missing required field '{field_name}'"
213	                    )
214	
215	            # GitHub URL format
216	            if entry.remote and not GITHUB_URL_RE.match(entry.remote):
217	                result.ok = False
218	                entry_failed = True
219	                result.messages.append(
220	                    f"Entry '{entry.name}': remote URL '{entry.remote}' is not a valid GitHub URL"
221	                )
222	
223	            # GitHub reachability (only if URL format is valid)
224	            if entry.remote and GITHUB_URL_RE.match(entry.remote):
225	                if not self._github.is_reachable(entry.remote):
226	                    result.ok = False
227	                    entry_failed = True
228	                    result.messages.append(
229	                        f"Entry '{entry.name}': remote URL '{entry.remote}' is unreachable "
230	                        f"(repository not found or connection refused)"
231	                    )
232	
233	            if not entry_failed:
234	                result.validated.append(entry.name)
235	                result.messages.append(f"Entry '{entry.name}': valid")
236	            else:
237	                result.failed.append(entry.name)
238	
239	        # 3. repos_dir consistency checks
240	        if repos_dir is not None and repos_dir.exists():
241	            declared_names = {e.name for e in manifest.entries}
242	            actual_dirs = {d.name for d in repos_dir.iterdir() if d.is_dir()}
243	
244	            # Extra dirs not in manifest
245	            extra = actual_dirs - declared_names
246	            for name in sorted(extra):
247	                result.ok = False
248	                result.messages.append(
249	                    f"Repos directory contains unexpected entry '{name}' not in manifest"
250	                )
251	
252	            # Missing clones
253	            for entry in manifest.entries:
254	                clone_path = repos_dir / entry.name
255	                if not clone_path.exists():
256	                    result.ok = False
257	                    result.messages.append(
258	                        f"Entry '{entry.name}': declared in manifest but no local clone found"
259	                    )
260	                elif clone_path.is_dir():
261	                    # Check remote mismatch
262	                    actual_url = self._git.get_remote_url(clone_path)
263	                    if actual_url is not None and actual_url != entry.remote:
264	                        result.ok = False
265	                        result.messages.append(
266	                            f"Entry '{entry.name}': remote URL mismatch — "
267	                            f"manifest declares '{entry.remote}' but clone has '{actual_url}'"
268	                        )
269	
270	        return result
271	
272	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
273	    # list
274	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
275	
276	    def list_bcs(self, manifest_path: Path) -> tuple[int, str]:
277	        """
278	        Return (exit_code, output) listing one BC per line.
279	
280	        Format per line: <canonical-name> <remote-url>
281	        Exits zero if manifest is valid; non-zero on parse error.
282	        """
283	        try:
284	            manifest = load_manifest(manifest_path)
285	        except (yaml.YAMLError, ValueError) as exc:
286	            return 1, f"Error loading manifest: {exc}\n"
287	
288	        lines = [f"{entry.name} {entry.remote}" for entry in manifest.entries]
289	        return 0, "\n".join(lines) + ("\n" if lines else "")
290	
291	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
292	    # sync
293	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
294	
295	    def sync(
296	        self,
297	        manifest_path: Path,
298	        repos_dir: Path,
299	    ) -> tuple[int, str]:
300	        """
301	        Sync repos_dir against the manifest.
302	
303	        - Clone missing repos.
304	        - Skip existing repos with matching remote.
305	        - Warn about repos_dir entries not in manifest (but do not delete them).
306	
307	        Returns (exit_code, output).
308	        """
309	        try:
310	            manifest = load_manifest(manifest_path)
311	        except (yaml.YAMLError, ValueError) as exc:
312	            return 1, f"Error loading manifest: {exc}\n"
313	
314	        repos_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
315	        declared_names = {e.name for e in manifest.entries}
316	
317	        lines: list[str] = []
318	        clone_count = 0
319	
320	        for entry in manifest.entries:
321	            dest = repos_dir / entry.name
322	            if dest.exists() and dest.is_dir():
323	                actual_url = self._git.get_remote_url(dest)
324	                if actual_url == entry.remote:
325	                    lines.append(
326	                        f"Skipped '{entry.name}': already present with matching remote"
327	                    )
328	                else:
329	                    lines.append(
330	                        f"Skipped '{entry.name}': already present (remote mismatch — "
331	                        f"expected '{entry.remote}', found '{actual_url}')"
332	                    )
333	            else:
334	                self._git.clone(entry.remote, dest)
335	                clone_count += 1
336	                lines.append(f"Cloned '{entry.name}' from '{entry.remote}'")
337	
338	        # Warn about extra dirs
339	        if repos_dir.exists():
340	            actual_dirs = {d.name for d in repos_dir.iterdir() if d.is_dir()}
341	            extra = actual_dirs - declared_names
342	            for name in sorted(extra):
343	                lines.append(f"Warning: '{name}' is present in repos dir but not declared in manifest")
344	
345	        return 0, "\n".join(lines) + ("\n" if lines else "")
346
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>

 `./repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher/src/bc_launcher/controller.py`

```text
1	"""
2	Business logic for bc-container subcommands.
3	
4	All Docker interaction goes through the DockerDriver interface, making this
5	layer fully testable without a live Docker daemon.
6	"""
7	from __future__ import annotations
8	
9	import os
10	import sys
11	from dataclasses import dataclass
12	
13	from bc_launcher.driver import ContainerMount, DockerDriver
14	
15	
16	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
17	# Constants / defaults
18	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
19	
20	CONTAINER_WORKSPACE = "/workspace"
21	AGENT_TMUX_SESSION = "agent"
22	BC_IMAGE = "ghcr.io/shopsystem/bc-base:latest"
23	SHOPMSG_DSN_ENV = "SHOPMSG_DSN"
24	
25	
26	def _container_name(bc_name: str) -> str:
27	    return f"bc-{bc_name}"
28	
29	
30	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
31	# Result type returned by commands that produce output
32	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
33	
34	@dataclass
35	class CommandResult:
36	    exit_code: int
37	    stdout: str
38	    stderr: str = ""
39	
40	
41	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
42	# Subcommand implementations
43	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
44	
45	class BcContainerController:
46	    """
47	    Pure-Python controller for bc-container operations.
48	
49	    Accepts a DockerDriver at construction time so tests can inject fakes.
50	    """
51	
52	    def __init__(self, driver: DockerDriver) -> None:
53	        self._driver = driver
54	
55	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
56	    # launch
57	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
58	
59	    def launch(
60	        self,
61	        bc_name: str,
62	        repo_url: str | None = None,
63	        shopmsg_dsn: str | None = None,
64	        startup_prompt: str | None = None,
65	        network: str | None = None,
66	    ) -> CommandResult:
67	        """
68	        Start a Docker container for the named BC.
69	
70	        Idempotent: if the container is already running, report and exit 0.
71	        """
72	        container = _container_name(bc_name)
73	
74	        if self._driver.is_running(container):
75	            return CommandResult(
76	                exit_code=0,
77	                stdout=f"{container} is already running\n",
78	            )
79	
80	        # Build environment
81	        env: dict[str, str] = {}
82	        if shopmsg_dsn:
83	            env[SHOPMSG_DSN_ENV] = shopmsg_dsn
84	        elif dsn := os.environ.get(SHOPMSG_DSN_ENV):
85	            env[SHOPMSG_DSN_ENV] = dsn
86	
87	        # Mounts: only the BC's own workspace mount + optional SHOPMSG socket
88	        mounts: list[tuple[str, str, str]] = []
89	
90	        # SHOPMSG_DSN may be a postgres DSN (no socket mount needed) or a
91	        # unix socket path.  If the DSN value looks like a socket file, add a
92	        # bind mount for it.
93	        dsn_value = env.get(SHOPMSG_DSN_ENV, "")
94	        if dsn_value.startswith("/") and not dsn_value.startswith("//"):
95	            # It's a host socket path — mount the containing directory
96	            socket_dir = os.path.dirname(dsn_value)
97	            mounts.append(("bind", socket_dir, socket_dir))
98	
99	        self._driver.run(
100	            container_name=container,
101	            image=BC_IMAGE,
102	            env=env,
103	            mounts=mounts,
104	            network=network,
105	            detach=True,
106	        )
107	
108	        out_lines: list[str] = [f"Started container {container}\n"]
109	
110	        # Clone repository if URL provided
111	        if repo_url:
112	            clone_result = self._driver.exec_run(
113	                container,
114	                ["git", "clone", repo_url, CONTAINER_WORKSPACE],
115	            )
116	            if clone_result.returncode != 0:
117	                return CommandResult(
118	                    exit_code=1,
119	                    stdout="".join(out_lines),
120	                    stderr=f"git clone failed: {clone_result.stderr}",
121	                )
122	            out_lines.append(f"Cloned {repo_url} into {CONTAINER_WORKSPACE}\n")
123	
124	            # bd dolt pull
125	            bd_result = self._driver.exec_run(
126	                container,
127	                ["bd", "dolt", "pull"],
128	            )
129	            out_lines.append("Ran bd dolt pull\n")
130	
131	        # Start tmux session
132	        tmux_result = self._driver.exec_run(
133	            container,
134	            ["tmux", "new-session", "-d", "-s", AGENT_TMUX_SESSION],
135	        )
136	        out_lines.append(f"Started tmux session '{AGENT_TMUX_SESSION}'\n")
137	
138	        # Optional startup prompt
139	        if startup_prompt:
140	            self._driver.exec_run(
141	                container,
142	                ["tmux", "send-keys", "-t", AGENT_TMUX_SESSION,
143	                 startup_prompt, "Enter"],
144	            )
145	            out_lines.append(f"Injected startup prompt: {startup_prompt!r}\n")
146	
147	        return CommandResult(exit_code=0, stdout="".join(out_lines))
148	
149	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
150	    # attach
151	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
152	
153	    def attach(self, bc_name: str) -> None:
154	        """
155	        Attach to the BC container's tmux session interactively.
156	        Replaces the current process via exec.
157	        """
158	        container = _container_name(bc_name)
159	        self._driver.exec_interactive(
160	            container,
161	            ["tmux", "attach-session", "-t", AGENT_TMUX_SESSION],
162	        )
163	
164	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
165	    # inject
166	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
167	
168	    def inject(self, bc_name: str, prompt_text: str) -> CommandResult:
169	        """Send text to the container's tmux session."""
170	        container = _container_name(bc_name)
171	        self._driver.exec_run(
172	            container,
173	            ["tmux", "send-keys", "-t", AGENT_TMUX_SESSION, prompt_text, "Enter"],
174	        )
175	        return CommandResult(
176	            exit_code=0,
177	            stdout=f"Sent {prompt_text!r} to {AGENT_TMUX_SESSION} in {container}\n",
178	        )
179	
180	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
181	    # monitor
182	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
183	
184	    def monitor(self, bc_name: str) -> CommandResult:
185	        """Capture and return the current contents of the tmux session pane."""
186	        container = _container_name(bc_name)
187	        result = self._driver.exec_run(
188	            container,
189	            ["tmux", "capture-pane", "-p", "-t", AGENT_TMUX_SESSION],
190	        )
191	        return CommandResult(
192	            exit_code=result.returncode,
193	            stdout=result.stdout,
194	            stderr=result.stderr,
195	        )
196	
197	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
198	    # stop
199	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
200	
201	    def stop(self, bc_name: str) -> CommandResult:
202	        """Stop and remove the BC container."""
203	        container = _container_name(bc_name)
204	        self._driver.stop(container)
205	        return CommandResult(exit_code=0, stdout=f"Stopped {container}\n")
206	
207	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
208	    # status
209	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
210	
211	    def status(self, bc_name: str) -> CommandResult:
212	        """Report running state of the BC container and its tmux session."""
213	        container = _container_name(bc_name)
214	        is_running = self._driver.is_running(container)
215	
216	        if not is_running:
217	            return CommandResult(
218	                exit_code=0,
219	                stdout=(
220	                    f"bc_name: {bc_name}\n"
221	                    f"container: {container}\n"
222	                    f"container_state: stopped\n"
223	                ),
224	            )
225	
226	        # Check tmux session
227	        tmux_result = self._driver.exec_run(
228	            container,
229	            ["tmux", "has-session", "-t", AGENT_TMUX_SESSION],
230	        )
231	        tmux_state = "active" if tmux_result.returncode == 0 else "inactive"
232	
233	        return CommandResult(
234	            exit_code=0,
235	            stdout=(
236	                f"bc_name: {bc_name}\n"
237	                f"container: {container}\n"
238	                f"container_state: running\n"
239	                f"tmux_session: {tmux_state}\n"
240	            ),
241	        )
242	
243	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
244	    # list
245	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
246	
247	    def list_containers(self) -> CommandResult:
248	        """List all known BC containers with their states."""
249	        infos = self._driver.list_bc_containers()
250	        if not infos:
251	            return CommandResult(exit_code=0, stdout="No BC containers found.\n")
252	
253	        lines: list[str] = []
254	        for info in infos:
255	            # Derive bc_name by stripping leading "bc-"
256	            bc_name = info.name.removeprefix("bc-")
257	            state = "running" if info.running else "stopped"
258	            lines.append(f"{bc_name}: {state}\n")
259	
260	        return CommandResult(exit_code=0, stdout="".join(lines))
261	
262	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
263	    # isolation check (used by tests and the isolate-check subcommand)
264	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
265	
266	    def get_bind_mounts(self, container_name: str) -> list[ContainerMount]:
267	        """Return only bind-type mounts for a running container."""
268	        all_mounts = self._driver.get_mounts(container_name)
269	        return [m for m in all_mounts if m.type == "bind"]
270	
271	    def last_command(self) -> list[str]:
272	        return self._driver.last_command()
273
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher/src/bc_launcher/driver.py`

```text
1	"""
2	Docker driver interface and real implementation.
3	
4	The DockerDriver protocol is the seam between bc_launcher business logic and
5	the real Docker daemon.  Tests inject a FakeDockerDriver; production code uses
6	RealDockerDriver which shells out to the docker CLI.
7	"""
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	import subprocess
11	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
12	from typing import Protocol
13	
14	
15	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
16	# Data types
17	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
18	
19	@dataclass
20	class ContainerMount:
21	    """Represents one mount entry for a running container."""
22	    type: str          # "bind", "volume", "tmpfs", …
23	    source: str        # host-side source path / volume name / socket path
24	    destination: str   # path inside the container
25	
26	
27	@dataclass
28	class ContainerInfo:
29	    """Aggregated state for a BC container."""
30	    name: str
31	    running: bool
32	    mounts: list[ContainerMount] = field(default_factory=list)
33	
34	
35	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
36	# Protocol
37	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
38	
39	class DockerDriver(Protocol):
40	    """Minimal Docker operations needed by bc_launcher."""
41	
42	    def is_running(self, container_name: str) -> bool:
43	        """Return True if the named container is currently running."""
44	        ...
45	
46	    def run(
47	        self,
48	        container_name: str,
49	        image: str,
50	        env: dict[str, str],
51	        mounts: list[tuple[str, str, str]],  # (type, source, dest)
52	        network: str | None,
53	        detach: bool,
54	    ) -> None:
55	        """Start a new container."""
56	        ...
57	
58	    def exec_run(self, container_name: str, command: list[str]) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:
59	        """Execute a command inside a running container."""
60	        ...
61	
62	    def exec_interactive(self, container_name: str, command: list[str]) -> None:
63	        """Execute an interactive command inside a running container (replaces process)."""
64	        ...
65	
66	    def stop(self, container_name: str) -> None:
67	        """Stop and remove the named container."""
68	        ...
69	
70	    def list_bc_containers(self) -> list[ContainerInfo]:
71	        """Return ContainerInfo for every container whose name starts with 'bc-'."""
72	        ...
73	
74	    def get_mounts(self, container_name: str) -> list[ContainerMount]:
75	        """Return the mount list for a running container."""
76	        ...
77	
78	    def last_command(self) -> list[str]:
79	        """Return the most recently built shell command (for test assertions)."""
80	        ...
81	
82	
83	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
84	# Real implementation (shells out to docker CLI)
85	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
86	
87	class RealDockerDriver:
88	    """Production DockerDriver that shells out to the docker CLI."""
89	
90	    def __init__(self) -> None:
91	        self._last_command: list[str] = []
92	
93	    def is_running(self, container_name: str) -> bool:
94	        result = subprocess.run(
95	            ["docker", "ps", "--filter", f"name=^{container_name}$",
96	             "--format", "{{.Names}}"],
97	            capture_output=True, text=True, check=False,
98	        )
99	        return container_name in result.stdout.split()
100	
101	    def run(
102	        self,
103	        container_name: str,
104	        image: str,
105	        env: dict[str, str],
106	        mounts: list[tuple[str, str, str]],
107	        network: str | None,
108	        detach: bool,
109	    ) -> None:
110	        cmd = ["docker", "run", "--name", container_name]
111	        if detach:
112	            cmd.append("-d")
113	        for key, val in env.items():
114	            cmd += ["-e", f"{key}={val}"]
115	        for mount_type, source, dest in mounts:
116	            cmd += ["--mount", f"type={mount_type},source={source},target={dest}"]
117	        if network:
118	            cmd += ["--network", network]
119	        cmd.append(image)
120	        self._last_command = cmd
121	        subprocess.run(cmd, check=True)
122	
123	    def exec_run(self, container_name: str, command: list[str]) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:
124	        cmd = ["docker", "exec", container_name] + command
125	        self._last_command = cmd
126	        return subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, check=False)
127	
128	    def exec_interactive(self, container_name: str, command: list[str]) -> None:
129	        import os
130	        cmd = ["docker", "exec", "-it", container_name] + command
131	        self._last_command = cmd
132	        os.execvp("docker", cmd)
133	
134	    def stop(self, container_name: str) -> None:
135	        cmd = ["docker", "rm", "-f", container_name]
136	        self._last_command = cmd
137	        subprocess.run(cmd, check=True)
138	
139	    def list_bc_containers(self) -> list[ContainerInfo]:
140	        # list all containers (running + stopped) whose name starts with bc-
141	        result = subprocess.run(
142	            ["docker", "ps", "-a", "--filter", "name=^bc-",
143	             "--format", "{{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}"],
144	            capture_output=True, text=True, check=False,
145	        )
146	        infos: list[ContainerInfo] = []
147	        for line in result.stdout.splitlines():
148	            if not line.strip():
149	                continue
150	            parts = line.split("\t", 1)
151	            name = parts[0].strip()
152	            status = parts[1].strip() if len(parts) > 1 else ""
153	            running = status.lower().startswith("up")
154	            infos.append(ContainerInfo(name=name, running=running))
155	        return infos
156	
157	    def get_mounts(self, container_name: str) -> list[ContainerMount]:
158	        result = subprocess.run(
159	            ["docker", "inspect", "--format",
160	             "{{range .Mounts}}{{.Type}}\t{{.Source}}\t{{.Destination}}\n{{end}}",
161	             container_name],
162	            capture_output=True, text=True, check=False,
163	        )
164	        mounts: list[ContainerMount] = []
165	        for line in result.stdout.splitlines():
166	            parts = line.split("\t")
167	            if len(parts) == 3:
168	                mounts.append(ContainerMount(
169	                    type=parts[0].strip(),
170	                    source=parts[1].strip(),
171	                    destination=parts[2].strip(),
172	                ))
173	        return mounts
174	
175	    def last_command(self) -> list[str]:
176	        return self._last_command
177
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	Feature: bc-container derives Docker network name from bc-manifest.yaml product field

3	  Background:

7	  @scenario_hash:pending @bc:shopsystem-bc-launcher

9	    Given a bc-manifest.yaml exists containing:
10	      """
11	      product: shopsystem product
12	      bcs:
13	        - name: shopsystem-messaging
14	          remote: https://github.com/dstengle/shopsystem-messaging.git
15	          role: bc
16	      """

18	    And no explicit "--network" flag is provided
19	    When I run bc-container launch with BC name "shopsystem-messaging"
20	    Then the command exits zero
21	    And the FakeDockerDriver records that the docker run command for "bc-shopsystem-messaging" includes the flag "--network shopsystem-product"
22	
23	  @scenario_hash:pending @bc:shopsystem-bc-launcher
24	  Scenario: network name derivation slugifies the product field by lowercasing and replacing spaces with hyphens
25	    Given a bc-manifest.yaml exists with product field "My Ecommerce Shop"
26	    And no Docker container named "bc-shopsystem-messaging" is running
27	    And no explicit "--network" flag is provided
28	    When I run bc-container launch with BC name "shopsystem-messaging"

30	
31	  @scenario_hash:pending @bc:shopsystem-bc-launcher
32	  Scenario: bc-container launch exits non-zero when no bc-manifest.yaml exists and no --network flag is provided
33	    Given no bc-manifest.yaml exists in the working directory
34	    And no explicit "--network" flag is provided
35	    When I run bc-container launch with BC name "shopsystem-messaging"
36	    Then the command exits non-zero
37	    And stderr includes the text "no network: bc-manifest.yaml not found and --network not provided"
38
```
