```text
1	"""
2	FakeDockerDriver — in-memory test double for DockerDriver.
3	
4	Records calls and returns pre-configured state.  Tests set up state before
5	running the controller under test, then assert on the recorded calls.
6	"""
7	from __future__ import annotations
8	
9	import subprocess
10	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
11	
12	from bc_launcher.driver import ContainerInfo, ContainerMount
13	
14	
15	@dataclass
16	class ExecCall:
17	    """Records one exec_run or exec_interactive call."""
18	    container: str
19	    command: list[str]
20	
21	
22	class FakeDockerDriver:
23	    """
24	    Fully in-memory DockerDriver for tests.
25	
26	    State is pre-configured by setting attributes before the test action runs.
27	    """
28	
29	    def __init__(self) -> None:
30	        # Set of currently 'running' containers by name
31	        self._running: set[str] = set()
32	
33	        # Canned tmux session map: container_name -> set of session names
34	        self._tmux_sessions: dict[str, set[str]] = {}
35	
36	        # Canned tmux pane content: container_name -> pane text
37	        self._tmux_pane: dict[str, str] = {}
38	
39	        # Recorded exec calls
40	        self.exec_calls: list[ExecCall] = []
41	
42	        # Recorded interactive exec calls
43	        self.interactive_calls: list[ExecCall] = []
44	
45	        # Last run command (updated by every operation)
46	        self._last_command: list[str] = []
47	
48	        # Last docker run command specifically (only updated by run(), not exec_run())
49	        self._last_run_command: list[str] = []
50	
51	        # Canned mounts per container
52	        self._mounts: dict[str, list[ContainerMount]] = {}
53	
54	        # All known containers (includes stopped), used by list_bc_containers
55	        self._all_containers: dict[str, bool] = {}  # name -> running
56	
57	    # --- Setup helpers (called by step definitions) ---
58	
59	    def set_running(self, container_name: str, running: bool = True) -> None:
60	        if running:
61	            self._running.add(container_name)
62	            self._all_containers[container_name] = True
63	        else:
64	            self._running.discard(container_name)
65	            self._all_containers[container_name] = False
66	
67	    def add_tmux_session(self, container_name: str, session_name: str) -> None:
68	        self._tmux_sessions.setdefault(container_name, set()).add(session_name)
69	
70	    def set_tmux_pane_content(self, container_name: str, content: str) -> None:
71	        self._tmux_pane[container_name] = content
72	
73	    def set_mounts(self, container_name: str, mounts: list[ContainerMount]) -> None:
74	        self._mounts[container_name] = mounts
75	
76	    # --- DockerDriver protocol implementation ---
77	
78	    def is_running(self, container_name: str) -> bool:
79	        return container_name in self._running
80	
81	    def run(
82	        self,
83	        container_name: str,
84	        image: str,
85	        env: dict[str, str],
86	        mounts: list[tuple[str, str, str]],
87	        network: str | None,
88	        detach: bool,
89	    ) -> None:
90	        cmd = ["docker", "run", "--name", container_name]
91	        if detach:
92	            cmd.append("-d")
93	        for key, val in env.items():
94	            cmd += ["-e", f"{key}={val}"]
95	        for mount_type, source, dest in mounts:
96	            cmd += ["--mount", f"type={mount_type},source={source},target={dest}"]
97	        if network:
98	            cmd += ["--network", network]
99	        cmd.append(image)
100	        self._last_command = cmd
101	        self._last_run_command = cmd
102	        # Mark as running and record configured mounts
103	        self._running.add(container_name)
104	        self._all_containers[container_name] = True
105	        # Convert mount tuples to ContainerMount objects and store
106	        mount_objs = [
107	            ContainerMount(type=t, source=s, destination=d)
108	            for t, s, d in mounts
109	        ]
110	        self._mounts[container_name] = mount_objs
111	
112	    def exec_run(
113	        self, container_name: str, command: list[str]
114	    ) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:
115	        self.exec_calls.append(ExecCall(container=container_name, command=command))
116	        self._last_command = ["docker", "exec", container_name] + command
117	
118	        # Simulate tmux has-session
119	        if command[:3] == ["tmux", "has-session", "-t"]:
120	            session = command[3] if len(command) > 3 else ""
121	            sessions = self._tmux_sessions.get(container_name, set())
122	            rc = 0 if session in sessions else 1
123	            return subprocess.CompletedProcess(command, rc, "", "")
124	
125	        # Simulate tmux new-session
126	        if command[:3] == ["tmux", "new-session", "-d"]:
127	            session = command[command.index("-s") + 1] if "-s" in command else "default"
128	            self._tmux_sessions.setdefault(container_name, set()).add(session)
129	            return subprocess.CompletedProcess(command, 0, "", "")
130	
131	        # Simulate tmux capture-pane
132	        if command[:3] == ["tmux", "capture-pane", "-p"]:
133	            pane = self._tmux_pane.get(container_name, "")
134	            return subprocess.CompletedProcess(command, 0, pane, "")
135	
136	        # Simulate tmux send-keys
137	        if command[:2] == ["tmux", "send-keys"]:
138	            return subprocess.CompletedProcess(command, 0, "", "")
139	
140	        # Simulate git clone
141	        if command[0] == "git" and command[1] == "clone":
142	            return subprocess.CompletedProcess(command, 0, "", "")
143	
144	        # Simulate bd dolt pull
145	        if command[:3] == ["bd", "dolt", "pull"]:
146	            return subprocess.CompletedProcess(command, 0, "", "")
147	
148	        # Default: success
149	        return subprocess.CompletedProcess(command, 0, "", "")
150	
151	    def exec_interactive(self, container_name: str, command: list[str]) -> None:
152	        self.interactive_calls.append(ExecCall(container=container_name, command=command))
153	        self._last_command = ["docker", "exec", "-it", container_name] + command
154	
155	    def stop(self, container_name: str) -> None:
156	        self._last_command = ["docker", "rm", "-f", container_name]
157	        self._running.discard(container_name)
158	        self._all_containers[container_name] = False
159	
160	    def list_bc_containers(self) -> list[ContainerInfo]:
161	        return [
162	            ContainerInfo(name=name, running=running)
163	            for name, running in self._all_containers.items()
164	        ]
165	
166	    def get_mounts(self, container_name: str) -> list[ContainerMount]:
167	        return self._mounts.get(container_name, [])
168	
169	    def last_command(self) -> list[str]:
170	        return self._last_command
171	
172	    def last_run_command(self) -> list[str]:
173	        """Return the last docker run command (excludes exec_run / exec_interactive calls)."""
174	        return self._last_run_command
175
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
195	2. **The scenario carries the right tags** — the CLI's `--bc-tag` flag
196	   adds `@bc:<name>`; the CLI's hash-computation step adds

198	   tag by hand to the body file.
199	3. **The work_id is a lead beads issue ID** — see §6 of the spec. Single
200	   source of truth; flows outward from the lead shop.
201	
202	If a scenario fails the well-formed check, send it back to the PO for
203	sharpening; do not paper over the gap by adding context the BC has to
204	infer.
205	
206	## Sufficiency check — `request_bugfix`
207	
208	1. **The description names the behavior under change concretely.**
209	   Reference the prior scenario hash being tightened (if applicable).
210	2. **The description marks scope: additive vs superseded.** Clear which
211	   prior contracts continue to hold.
212	3. **If `scenarios` is non-empty**, each embedded scenario passes the
213	   `assign_scenarios` sufficiency check above.
214	4. **The work_id is a fresh lead beads issue** — even if this is a §4.4
215	   follow-up to a prior work_id, the bugfix gets its own ID.
216	
217	## Sufficiency check — `request_maintenance`
218	
219	1. **The description is concrete enough to act on.** Vague "improve X"
220	   framings will trigger the BC Implementer's clarify-default posture
221	   and waste a round trip.
222	2. **Acceptance criteria, when present, are measurable.** The BC
223	   Implementer template's S2c probe surfaced that vague criteria
224	   ("works correctly", "doesn't break things") trigger clarify
225	   regardless of the criteria field being populated. Pre-empt.
226	3. **File hints, when present, are accurate.** The BC will read them
227	   and treat them as authoritative.
228	
229	## Anti-rationalization
230	
231	When considering message-type selection, watch for these thoughts. Each
232	one is a pattern-match short-circuit instead of running the discriminator:
233	
234	- *"S8 was the vehicle for input-validation tightening, so this CLI work
235	  is also `request_bugfix`."* — STOP. Verify the BC's pre-state. Slice
236	  11 nearly produced this exact mistake; the user caught it. The
237	  discriminator is the pre-state, not the prior-slice pattern.
238	- *"`request_bugfix` carries scenarios too, so the choice doesn't matter
239	  much."* — STOP. The catalog message type names the *intent*. The
240	  Implementer's sufficiency check is calibrated per message type;
241	  picking the wrong one means the wrong check runs.
242	- *"I'll use `assign_scenarios` because the CLI is simpler."* — STOP.
243	  If the BC already has the behavior, framing it as new behavior gets
244	  the Implementer to accept it without flagging the duplication.
245	- *"It's a minor refactor, surely `request_maintenance` is fine."* —
246	  Check: is it really flat, or are there scenario tightenings implicit
247	  in the change? If implicit, it's `request_bugfix`.
248	- *"I read the code and the capability exists, so Q1 is yes."* — STOP.
249	  Reading is hypothesis; running is fact. Slice 16 produced exactly this
250	  failure: the Architect read the CLI code, asserted that the producer
251	  already maintained hash/gherkin consistency, and was wrong (the code
252	  computed `hash = canonical(body)`, but the gherkin field was
253	  `wrapped(body)` — different inputs to the canonicalization rule). The
254	  Implementer caught the mismatch and adapted; the round trip was wasted
255	  to the extent that the Implementer also had to rewrite a CLI function
--
```

The scenario body files are clean — just the `Scenario:` block, no Feature header, no tags. The PO-authored files at `/tmp/network-scenarios/` have Feature headers and `@scenario_hash:pending` tags. I need to extract just the scenario bodies into clean files. The `/tmp` files have multiple scenarios per file, so I need to split them by scenario, one per file.

Let me extract the 9 individual scenario bodies.

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-22 16:40:26Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Extract individual scenario bodies into clean files

```bash
mkdir -p /tmp/network-scenarios-clean
# Extract scenario bodies from 01-derive.gherkin (3 scenarios)
python3 << 'EOF'
import re

files = {
    '/tmp/network-scenarios/01-derive.gherkin': 3,
    '/tmp/network-scenarios/02-create.gherkin': 2,
    '/tmp/network-scenarios/03-override.gherkin': 2,
    '/tmp/network-scenarios/04-shared.gherkin': 2,
}

counters = {
    '/tmp/network-scenarios/01-derive.gherkin': range(1, 4),
    '/tmp/network-scenarios/02-create.gherkin': range(4, 6),
    '/tmp/network-scenarios/03-override.gherkin': range(6, 8),
    '/tmp/network-scenarios/04-shared.gherkin': range(8, 10),
}

for path, nums in counters.items():
    with open(path) as f:
        content = f.read()
    
    # Split into scenario blocks - find lines starting with "  Scenario:"
    # Strip Background, Feature header, and @scenario_hash tags
    lines = content.split('\n')
    
    scenarios = []
    current = []
    in_scenario = False
    
    for line in lines:
        stripped = line.strip()
        if stripped.startswith('@scenario_hash:') or stripped.startswith('@bc:'):
            # Skip tag lines before scenarios
            continue
        if stripped.startswith('Scenario:'):
            if current:
                scenarios.append('\n'.join(current).rstrip())
            current = [line]
            in_scenario = True
        elif in_scenario:
            # Skip Feature/Background lines
            if stripped.startswith('Feature:') or stripped.startswith('Background:') or stripped.startswith('Given the shopsystem') or stripped.startswith('And the FakeDockerDriver'):
                continue
            current.append(line)
    
    if current:
        scenarios.append('\n'.join(current).rstrip())
    
    # Filter out empty/whitespace-only
    scenarios = [s for s in scenarios if s.strip()]
    print(f"{path}: found {len(scenarios)} scenarios")
    for i, (num, body) in enumerate(zip(nums, scenarios)):
        fname = f'/tmp/network-scenarios-clean/{num:02d}.gherkin'
        with open(fname, 'w') as f:
            f.write(body + '\n')
        print(f"  -> {fname}")
        print(f"     {body[:100]!r}")

EOF
```

```text
/tmp/network-scenarios/01-derive.gherkin: found 3 scenarios
  -> /tmp/network-scenarios-clean/01.gherkin
```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-22 16:40:51Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Re-extract scenario bodies properly without filtering content lines

```bash
python3 << 'EOF'
import re

# Map: source file -> list of (output_filename, scenario_number)
file_map = [
    ('/tmp/network-scenarios/01-derive.gherkin', [1, 2, 3]),
    ('/tmp/network-scenarios/02-create.gherkin', [4, 5]),
    ('/tmp/network-scenarios/03-override.gherkin', [6, 7]),
    ('/tmp/network-scenarios/04-shared.gherkin', [8, 9]),
]

for src_path, nums in file_map:
    with open(src_path) as f:
        lines = f.readlines()
    
    scenarios = []
    current = None
    in_docstring = False
    docstring_indent = None
    
    i = 0
    while i < len(lines):
        line = lines[i]
        stripped = line.rstrip()
        stripped_lstrip = stripped.lstrip()
        
        # Toggle docstring
        if '"""' in stripped:
            if not in_docstring:
                in_docstring = True
                if current is not None:
                    current.append(stripped)
                i += 1
                continue
            else:
                in_docstring = False
                if current is not None:
                    current.append(stripped)
                i += 1
                continue
        
        if in_docstring:
            if current is not None:
                current.append(stripped)
            i += 1
            continue
        
        # Skip Feature:, Background:, @scenario_hash lines, @bc: lines
        if stripped_lstrip.startswith('Feature:') or \
           stripped_lstrip.startswith('Background:') or \
           stripped_lstrip.startswith('@scenario_hash:') or \
           stripped_lstrip.startswith('@bc:'):
            i += 1
            continue
        
        # Skip Background steps (the two Given/And lines after Background:)
        # We handle this by tracking when we're in the Background block
        # Actually - let's just detect Scenario: to start collecting
        
        if stripped_lstrip.startswith('Scenario:'):
            if current is not None:
                scenarios.append('\n'.join(current).rstrip())
            current = [stripped]
            i += 1
            continue
        
        if current is not None:
            # Skip blank lines at start of scenario
            if stripped_lstrip == '' and len(current) == 1:
                i += 1
                continue
            current.append(stripped)
        # else: we're in Feature header / Background - skip
        
        i += 1
    
    if current is not None and current:
        scenarios.append('\n'.join(current).rstrip())
    
    print(f"{src_path}: {len(scenarios)} scenarios")
    for num, body in zip(nums, scenarios):
        out = f'/tmp/network-scenarios-clean/{num:02d}.gherkin'
        with open(out, 'w') as f:
            f.write(body + '\n')
        # Print first and last line
        blines = body.split('\n')
        print(f"  {out}: {len(blines)} lines | first: {blines[0][:80]!r} | last: {blines[-1][:80]!r}")

EOF
```

```text
Get shop-msg watch help
--
284	### Sending a message to a BC via shop-msg send
285	
286	1. **Identify the work.** New capability, tightening, or flat change?
287	2. **Apply the message-type sufficiency check** above. Verify the BC's
288	   pre-state before picking a vehicle.
289	3. **Apply the per-message-type sufficiency check** for the vehicle you
290	   picked (above).
291	4. **Compose via `shop-msg send <type>`** with the appropriate flags.
292	   For `assign_scenarios` and `request_bugfix` that carry scenarios,
293	   prepare scenario body files (no Feature line, no tags) and pass via
294	   repeatable `--scenario-file`; the CLI handles hashing and wrapping.
295	5. **Verify the message was deposited** by reading it back via
296	   `shop-msg read inbox --bc-root <BC root> --work-id <work_id>`.
297	   Confirm the scenario hashes match what `scenarios hash` produces
298	   for the bodies. To see at a glance which BCs currently have pending
299	   outbound responses to your lead-shop, run
300	   `shop-msg pending outbox --lead-root <lead root>`.
301	6. **Report** which vehicle you selected, which sufficiency-check question
302	   made the call, the work_id, and the scenario hashes (if any).
303	
304	### Responding to a BC clarify via shop-msg respond
305	
306	1. **Read the clarify** from the BC's outbox via `shop-msg read outbox
307	   --bc-root <BC root> --work-id <work_id>`. The `shop-msg` CLI is the
308	   messaging BC's boundary; do not bypass it to read outbox storage by
309	   other means.
310	2. **Verify the clarify is yours.** Architecture / decomposition / contract
311	   questions route to you; scope and vocabulary route to the PO. If
```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-22 16:42:00Z)**_

Done. Here is the report.

---

**Empirical pre-state — Q1 findings**

`grep -n "product|network_create|slugify|derive"` across all three source files returned:
- `manifest.py`: zero matches. `Manifest` and `BcEntry` dataclasses have no `product` field; `REQUIRED_FIELDS` is `("name", "remote", "role")`.
- `controller.py`: zero matches. `BcContainerController.launch()` accepts a `network: str | None` parameter but derives nothing — it passes the value straight through to `driver.run()`.
- `driver.py`: zero matches. `DockerDriver` protocol has no `network_create` or `network_exists` method; `FakeDockerDriver` similarly absent.

Capability confirmed absent. Vehicle: `assign_scenarios`.

**Scenario review notes**

All 9 scenarios are well-formed. One technical note included in the dispatch description: `FakeDockerDriver` exists at `tests/fake_driver.py` and is the established pattern (`conftest.py` already imports it), but it has no `network_create` / `network_exists` methods — the implementer must add those to both the `DockerDriver` protocol and `FakeDockerDriver` as part of this work. No existing scenario hash in `bc_container.feature` or `bc_manifest.feature` covers network derivation.

**Dispatch summary**

- work_id: `lead-e6j`
- BC: `shopsystem-bc-launcher`
- Feature title: `bc-container product-scoped Docker network naming`
- Scenarios: 9 deposited

**Scenario hashes (from inbox read-back)**

| # | Description | Hash |
|---|-------------|------|
| 01 | Derives network from product field | `229760feb4af874b` |
| 02 | Slugifies product field (My Ecommerce Shop → my-ecommerce-shop) | `b0861d05f82fd0f2` |
| 03 | Exits non-zero with no manifest and no --network | `f1c3a6ca6b64b713` |
| 04 | Creates network before container when absent | `add8efc2668d1cdc` |
| 05 | Skips create when network exists | `7bfab6f4f71e5a6e` |
| 06 | --network overrides derived name | `3cb6e3c0c8d8235e` |
| 07 | --network suppresses auto-create | `dde337597985c122` |
| 08 | Two containers share derived network | `efc2032059b5c8e5` |
| 09 | Network create called only once across two launches | `b48bb2794a952a99` |

Inbox verified — all 9 scenarios present with correct tags. Scenario files committed to `features/bc-launcher/` (18–26), beads pushed, git pushed.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-22 16:42:07Z)**_

Dispatched. Summary of what the bc-launcher will implement:

**`lead-e6j` — 9 scenarios assigned to `shopsystem-bc-launcher`**

New additions required in the BC:
- `bc-manifest.yaml` schema: new `product` field
- `controller.py`: network derivation logic (slugify `product` → network name, `docker network create` before `docker run` when absent)
- `driver.py`: new `network_create` / `network_exists` methods on both the `DockerDriver` protocol and `FakeDockerDriver`

The `--network` flag continues to work as a full override; the derived name is the default when the flag is omitted; missing manifest + no flag = non-zero exit with diagnostic.

Waiting on the BC to return `work_done` on `lead-e6j`.
